elements.text: (ii) Quality right party contact EDR reporting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must notify Freddie Mac that it achieved quality right party contact with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower via an EDR transmission within the first three Business Days of the month as
elements.label: text

elements.text: follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Report default action code AW and the date of the first quality right party contact
elements.label: text

elements.text: established with the Borrower. Report the date one time in the month following the
elements.label: text

elements.text: month that quality right party contact was established with the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Report default action code AX if any subsequent quality right party contact was made
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the Borrower after the initial contact was established. Report the date of the last
elements.label: text

elements.text: quality right party contact one time in the month following the month that the action
elements.label: text

elements.text: occurred. Default action code AX may be reported in the same month as default
elements.label: text

elements.text: action code AW if a Servicer establishes a subsequent quality right party contact with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower in the same month that initial contact was achieved. If the Servicer is
elements.label: text

elements.text: working with the Borrower over a period of several months to resolve the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency, it must continue to report default action code AX with the date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: last quality right party contact for each month in which the action applies
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Single point of contact
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower outreach and solicitation requirements specified in this chapter represent
elements.label: text

elements.text: the minimum required effort a Servicer must make to reach Borrowers early in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency process and throughout the Delinquency cycle to more efficiently mitigate
elements.label: text

elements.text: default. Freddie Mac encourages and fully supports Servicers that have or will implement
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional processes, such as a single point of contact model, not currently required under
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Guide or Servicers’ other Purchase Documents for purposes of achieving contact
elements.label: text

elements.text: continuity throughout the Delinquency process.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer elects to have a process supporting a single point of contact model and is
elements.label: text

elements.text: using a team approach, the Servicer should provide the Borrower the ability to request
elements.label: text

elements.text: and speak to a specific person from the assigned personnel team and leave a message.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, the Servicer may not use any additional processes that are not compliant with
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law or otherwise adverse to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9102.4: Servicer collection efforts (12/01/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All collection efforts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Minimum collection efforts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special Servicing, Early Delinquency Counseling and NextJob® re-employment services
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Investment Property and rental income during Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) All collection efforts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must treat each Delinquency individually by varying the collection techniques
elements.label: text

elements.text: to fit the individual circumstances. The Servicer must avoid establishing a fixed routine,
elements.label: text

elements.text: which is particularly ineffective when dealing with chronically delinquent Borrowers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All collection efforts must be based on the extent of the Delinquency and the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: knowledge of the following factors:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower’s prior payment history
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower’s credit history
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower’s employment situation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower’s reason for default
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower’s willingness and ability to repay
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower is deceased or the property is included as part of the Borrower’s estate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgaged Premises’ condition
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgaged Premises’ occupancy status
elements.label: text

elements.text: If there is a known potential risk of loss or ownership to Freddie Mac, the Borrower must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: referred for loss mitigation immediately.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers should encourage Borrowers to set up Automated Clearing House (ACH) payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: arrangements. If a Borrower agreed to have his or her monthly payments automatically
elements.label: text

elements.text: withdrawn from a bank account, the Servicer must ensure that the correct monthly payment is
elements.label: text

elements.text: withdrawn (especially after an Escrow analysis has taken place) and processed timely. If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: ACH payment cannot be processed on the specified date the Borrower has agreed to, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must begin efforts to contact the Borrower within 48 hours to determine the reason
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the missed payment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Minimum collection efforts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must, at a minimum:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be readily available to the Borrower to offer financial counseling and advice on curing
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Delinquency and explaining alternatives to foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Make personal contact with the Borrower as early and often as necessary to promptly
elements.label: text

elements.text: cure the Delinquency. (Mortgages in Bankruptcy or litigation may be excluded if
elements.label: text

elements.text: necessary under applicable law, rules of professional responsibility or court rules.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are authorized to use alternative outreach methods to contact the Borrower as
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitted by applicable law including, but not limited to, e-mail, text messaging or voice
elements.label: text

elements.text: response unit technology.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Continue to contact the Borrower if satisfactory arrangements have not been made to cure
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Delinquency or until the Servicer determines foreclosure is appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Issue the breach letter (may also be referred to as the “notice of acceleration” or “demand
elements.label: text

elements.text: letter”) for all Mortgages no later than the 75th day of Delinquency. If State law requires
elements.label: text

elements.text: the breach letter (or any other pre-breach letter) to be sent prior to the 75th day of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency in order to be able to refer the Mortgage to foreclosure by the 120th day of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency (i.e., 150 days after the DDLPI), the Servicer must send the breach letter on
elements.label: text

elements.text: such earlier date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer determines the property is vacant or abandoned pursuant to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9202.3(c)(iii), then the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Issue a breach letter within 10 days of the determination, provided the Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: greater than 30 days delinquent; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Refer the Mortgage to foreclosure after expiration of the breach letter, unless the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer establishes quality right party contact with the Borrower to resolve the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency. Regardless of whether quality right party contact is achieved, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage must be referred to foreclosure no later than the 120th day of Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: unless one of the exceptions to postpone foreclosure referral described in Sections
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9301.2(c) and 9301.2(d) applies.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Contact the lessor for leasehold Mortgages and any lender with a prior lien on the fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: simple landowner/lessor’s fee simple interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Continue skip trace efforts until all reasonable sources have been attempted or contact
elements.label: text

elements.text: numbers and addresses have been verified
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: set forth in Section 9201.5.

elements.text: (c) Special Servicing, Early Delinquency Counseling and NextJob® re-employment services
elements.label: text

elements.text: The requirements for special Servicing, Early Delinquency Counseling and NextJob® re￾employment services, as described in this section, apply to Freddie Mac Home Possible®
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages (see Chapter 4501).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Special Servicing — Welcome Letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must send the Borrower a letter, shortly after closing and before the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage payment is due, stressing the importance of making timely payments and
elements.label: text

elements.text: advising the Borrower to contact the Servicer if he or she experiences any financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: problems. The letter may be incorporated into the welcome letter sent to Borrowers after
elements.label: text

elements.text: closing. The letter must include the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Instructions on how to contact the Servicer if the Borrower is having difficulty
elements.label: text

elements.text: making the Mortgage payment on time
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer’s business hours
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A toll-free telephone number to reach the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Early Delinquency Counseling
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following provisions apply to Early Delinquency Counseling:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Early Delinquency Counseling is counseling provided to a delinquent Borrower by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: nonprofit third-party homeownership-counseling agency or an eligible Servicer that
elements.label: text

elements.text: involves identifying the reason(s) a Borrower did not make a Mortgage payment on
elements.label: text

elements.text: time and working with the Borrower to resolve any financial problems so that future
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage payments can be made on a timely basis. The counseling includes a
elements.label: text

elements.text: personal and interactive relationship with the Borrower that deals with money
elements.label: text

elements.text: management, budgeting and debt management counseling
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicers must offer Early Delinquency Counseling, including household budget
elements.label: text

elements.text: management counseling, at no charge to delinquent Borrowers for each Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: that occurs during the first year following the Note Date. In addition, if at any time
elements.label: text

elements.text: during the life of the Mortgage the Borrower is having difficulty making Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments, the Servicer should advise the Borrower to speak with the Servicer or
elements.label: text

elements.text: contact a counseling agency for further assistance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must provide counseling in accordance with at least one of the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: options:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A nonprofit third-party homeownership counseling agency
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A Servicer. The Servicer may conduct the counseling provided the Servicer has
elements.label: text

elements.text: policies and procedures in place to offer the same kind of comprehensive
elements.label: text

elements.text: counseling, budgeting and advising capabilities as a counseling agency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A HUD-approved national counseling agency specified by Freddie Mac. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may use this option to refer delinquent Borrowers with Home Possible
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages to Freddie Mac for counseling services provided by a national
elements.label: text

elements.text: counseling agency without charge to the Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To refer a delinquent Borrower for counseling services provided by an agency, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must complete the “Counseling Agency-Servicer Referral” template in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the instructions provided in the template and send the referral via
elements.label: text

elements.text: secure e-mail to Freddie Mac at FM_Counseling_Outreach@FreddieMac.com.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The template is accessible at https://sf.freddiemac.com/docs/xls/fact￾sheet/counseling_servicer_referral.xls. The template contains instructions for
elements.label: text

elements.text: carrying out referrals to the agencies and for identifying Mortgages that are
elements.label: text

elements.text: ineligible for referral. After Freddie Mac receives the referral, Freddie Mac and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the agency will contact the Borrower to offer counseling services to the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For more information about the network of national counseling agencies,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers should visit the My Home by Freddie Mac website at
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://myhome.freddiemac.com/resources/housing-help-centers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicer relies on a counseling agency, it must be fully aware of the status or
elements.label: text

elements.text: outcome of all counseling efforts the counseling agency undertakes with a specific
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Initial contact with delinquent Borrower. If the Borrower is experiencing a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Send a letter advising the Borrower of the availability of free counseling
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Contact the delinquent Borrower to determine the Borrower’s current financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: situation and the reason for the Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Counseling process and actions. The counseling must include the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Analyzing the Borrower’s financial situation and developing a plan of action for
elements.label: text

elements.text: solving the Delinquency, which in most cases will be a budget worksheet or
elements.label: text

elements.text: workout plan giving priority to the Mortgage payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Developing a budget and debt repayment plan enabling the Borrower to meet his
elements.label: text

elements.text: or her financial obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Reviewing the budget worksheet or workout plan with the Borrower and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer, if applicable, so a decision can be made on how to proceed
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In addition to minimum collection efforts set forth in this section, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: offer Early Delinquency Counseling no later than the 30th day after the Due Date and
elements.label: text

elements.text: schedule or conduct the initial counseling session with the Borrower no later than the
elements.label: text

elements.text: 45th day after the Due Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Servicer or a third-party non-profit homeownership counseling agency chosen
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Servicer provides the counseling, the Servicer must include in the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: file:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A copy of the “Welcome Letter” as described in Section 9102.4(c)(i)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The date(s) that counseling was offered
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Borrower’s response(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The name of the counseling agency providing the counseling (if not the Servicer)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A brief summary of the results of the counseling
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Servicer utilizes a HUD-approved national counseling agency specified by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac to provide the counseling, as outlined above, the Servicer must include
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Mortgage file:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A copy of the “Welcome Letter” as described in Section 9102.4(c)(i)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The results of the counseling outreach as provided by Freddie Mac or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified HUD national counseling agency, as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Mortgage is included in a Transfer of Servicing before the end of the one-year
elements.label: text

elements.text: period during which Early Delinquency Counseling is required, the transferee
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must be informed of the requirement and must be able to provide the
elements.label: text

elements.text: required counseling or make arrangements for a counseling agency, as necessary
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)NextJob re-employment services
elements.label: text

elements.text: Increasing homeownership opportunities in underserved markets across the nation,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including in rural and high-needs areas, is a key component of Freddie Mac’s Duty to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Serve plan. High-needs areas include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Middle Appalachia
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The lower Mississippi Delta
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Colonias
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other tracts located in areas subject to persistent poverty
elements.label: text

elements.text: As part of the Duty to Serve plan, Freddie Mac is providing an opportunity for distressed
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers with a Home Possible Mortgage who reside in a high-needs area to receive re￾employment services through NextJob. NextJob is a re-employment services company
elements.label: text

elements.text: that assists Borrowers with job search skills and training to increase the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: likelihood of re-employment after the loss of a job, reduced hours or other employment
elements.label: text

elements.text: challenges that threaten the Borrower’s ability to make timely mortgage payments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: NextJob will contact the Borrower and offer re-employment services, which include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ One-on-one job coaching
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Access to “Job Talk” webinars, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Access to NextJob’s proprietary online job search training program
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are encouraged to refer Borrowers with Home Possible Mortgages in Duty to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Serve high-needs areas (refer to Exhibit 40, Duty to Serve High-Needs Areas) who have
elements.label: text

elements.text: suffered a loss of income due to unemployment or underemployment to Freddie Mac for
elements.label: text

elements.text: referral to NextJob.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower qualifications appear in the table below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: NextJob re-employment services eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligibility Eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower must have:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Suffered a loss of income due to unemployment or
elements.label: text

elements.text: underemployment, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requested loss mitigation assistance from the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage must be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Located in a designated Duty to Serve high-needs area listed in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 40
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Home Possible Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: exclusions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following Borrowers are not eligible for NextJob re-employment
elements.label: text

elements.text: services:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrowers who are 12 months or more delinquent at the time of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer evaluation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrowers in active repayment plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrowers in active modification Trial Period Plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrowers approved for short sales or deeds-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrowers with Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Subject to active non-routine litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Subject to active bankruptcy proceedings
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ That are FHA, VA or RHS insured
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ With a foreclosure sale scheduled within the next 60 days, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ That have been referred to foreclosure if the parties are in
elements.label: text

elements.text: mediation
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower meets the eligibility criteria above, Servicers participating in the NextJob
elements.label: text

elements.text: program must provide the following information to Freddie Mac on a Microsoft Excel®
elements.label: text

elements.text: spreadsheet for each Borrower the Servicer is referring, in the format provided in Exhibit
elements.label: text

elements.text: 41, NextJob Referral Template for Borrowers with Home Possible Mortgages, which
elements.label: text

elements.text: includes the following information:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower name
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower phone number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower State of residence, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer contact name and e-mail address
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must send the Excel spreadsheet to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rural_Borrower_Help@FreddieMac.com up to twice per month only on the 15th or
elements.label: text

elements.text: 30th day of the month. Servicers with no eligible Borrowers for a particular month are
elements.label: text

elements.text: not required to make a submission.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Three to six Business Days after receiving the submission, Freddie Mac will e-mail the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer a list of Borrowers who were referred to NextJob.
elements.label: text

elements.text: NextJob will contact Borrowers by phone and work with them to complete the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Homeowner Re-employment Registration and Waiver Agreement to initiate the re￾employment services. NextJob will attempt to contact the Borrowers for 20 days.
elements.label: text

elements.text: NextJob will provide job skills services and ask the Borrower to complete a survey at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: end of the skills training.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will e-mail the Servicer monthly updates in a report entitled “Disposition of
elements.label: text

elements.text: NextJob Referrals.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Investment Property and rental income during Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: If satisfactory arrangements for repayment of the Delinquency have not been made, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must request that the Borrower consent to the appointment of a real estate agent or
elements.label: text

elements.text: other agent to collect rents, maintain the Mortgaged Premises and make payments to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer. If such arrangements are not made, the Servicer must determine what means are
elements.label: text

elements.text: available to obtain the rental income and submit a recommendation to Freddie Mac (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Directory 5).
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9102.5: Borrower solicitation and communication
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Glossary definitions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower solicitation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documents and forms
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Communications with the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Electronic submission of documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section sets forth Servicer communication, solicitation and notification requirements during
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Borrower’s Delinquency or upon receipt of a Borrower’s request for an alternative to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Glossary definitions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers should be familiar with Freddie Mac’s definitions for the following terms as they
elements.label: text

elements.text: relate to Borrower solicitation and communication:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Glossary definitions for Borrower solicitation and communication
elements.label: text

elements.text: B
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Evaluation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: The notice a Servicer must send to a Borrower once the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: completes its evaluation of the Borrower for an alternative to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Response
elements.label: text

elements.text: Package
elements.label: text

elements.text: The documents that must be submitted by a Borrower in order to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluated for certain alternatives to foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Solicitation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Package
elements.label: text

elements.text: The documents that must be sent to a delinquent Borrower informing
elements.label: text

elements.text: him or her of alternatives to foreclosure and the process and
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation required to be submitted to a Servicer in order to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluated for an alternative to foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Borrower preforeclosure solicitation
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer may not solicit a Borrower whose Mortgage is current or less than 31 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent (i.e., no more than one payment past due) for an alternative to foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Section 9201.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the minimum collection efforts set forth in Section 9102.4, Servicers may
elements.label: text

elements.text: begin soliciting Borrowers who are 31 or more days delinquent in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: processes and timelines set forth below in order to determine the reason for Delinquency and
elements.label: text

elements.text: solicit them for possible alternatives to foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicer previously determined that a Borrower who was less than 60 days delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: did not qualify for any alternative to foreclosure and the Borrower subsequently becomes 60
elements.label: text

elements.text: days delinquent, then the Servicer must continue its solicitation and collection efforts with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower in accordance with the requirements of this chapter. Continued solicitation may
elements.label: text

elements.text: include sending another Borrower Solicitation Package if documentation needs to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: refreshed in order to perform the subsequent evaluation of the Borrower for alternatives to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) No later than the 45th day after the Due Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must send at least one Borrower Solicitation Package or solicitation letter to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower with information, including but not limited to, the availability of alternatives to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure as set forth in Section 9102.5(c) below. See Section 9102.5(d) below for
elements.label: text

elements.text: specific information that must be included in the Borrower Solicitation Package.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer has not achieved quality right party contact and a resolution to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency, the Servicer is required to send at least one Borrower Solicitation Package
elements.label: text

elements.text: or solicitation letter to the delinquent Borrower no later than the 45th day after the Due
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date of an unpaid monthly installment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may send either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Borrower Solicitation Package, which includes a Borrower Solicitation Letter (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 1145, Freddie Mac Borrower Solicitation Letter); Form 710, Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assistance Application; frequently asked questions (FAQs) and foreclosure rescue
elements.label: text

elements.text: scam information
elements.label: text

elements.text: OR
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Borrower Solicitation Letter (see Exhibit 1145) and Form 710, FAQs and
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure rescue scam information upon establishing quality right party contact.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may also provide the FAQs and foreclosure rescue scam information on
elements.label: text

elements.text: its website and provide a link to that information in the Borrower Solicitation Letter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicer has achieved quality right party contact and has obtained from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower a resolution to the Delinquency, the Servicer is not required to send the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower Solicitation Package. However, in such instances, the Servicer must comply
elements.label: text

elements.text: with any early intervention notice that may be required under applicable law. If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower fails to perform under the conditions of a relief or workout option, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must resume collection efforts, including sending the Borrower Solicitation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Package.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) As early as the 90th day and no later than the 105th day after the Due Date, or, for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower with a Step-Rate Mortgage who becomes 60 days delinquent within 12
elements.label: text

elements.text: months following the first payment due date resulting from an interest rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: adjustment, as early as the 60th day and no later than the 75th day after the Due
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Borrower becomes 90 days delinquent, or when a Borrower has a Step-Rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage and becomes 60 days delinquent within the 12 months following the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment due date resulting from an interest rate adjustment, the Servicer must determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: if the Borrower is eligible for a streamlined offer for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification®
elements.label: text

elements.text: in accordance with Section 9206.1(c)(iii) and, if eligible, solicit the Borrower for such
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification. The Servicer must send an initial Exhibit 1191, Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification® Solicitation Cover Letter, and a Freddie Mac Flex Modification Trial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Period Plan Solicitation Offer – Not Based on an Evaluation of a BRP, included in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 93, Evaluation Notices, on or before the 105th day of Delinquency to a Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: who becomes 90 days delinquent and is otherwise eligible.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Borrower with a Step-Rate Mortgage who becomes 60 days delinquent and is
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise eligible, the Servicer must send an initial Exhibit 1191B, Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification® Solicitation Cover Letter for Day 60 Rate Reset, and a Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification Trial Period Plan Solicitation Offer – Not Based on an Evaluation of a BRP
elements.label: text

elements.text: on or before the 75th day of Delinquency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower has been approved for a streamlined offer for a Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification in accordance with the special requirements for Borrowers impacted by an
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible Disaster described in Section 9206.1(c)(v), the Servicer must send Exhibit
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1191A, Freddie Mac Flex Modification® Post-Disaster Forbearance Solicitation Cover
elements.label: text

elements.text: Letter, in place of Exhibit 1191. The Servicer must amend the applicable letter template
elements.label: text

elements.text: as necessary to conform to the Freddie Mac Flex Modification terms and requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Freddie Mac Flex Modification Solicitation Cover Letter and the Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification Trial Period Plan Solicitation Offer – Not Based on an Evaluation of a BRP
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be altered at the Servicer’s discretion as it deems necessary to meet the requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: of this section and Chapter 9206, and to comply with disclosure and other requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: under applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer fails to send the streamlined offer for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the 105th day of Delinquency to a Borrower who becomes 90 days delinquent and is
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise eligible, or by the 75th day of Delinquency to a Borrower with a Step-Rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage who becomes 60 days delinquent and is otherwise eligible, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: solicit eligible Borrowers as soon as possible to minimize the impacts of the delay. If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower does not respond to the initial Freddie Mac Flex Modification solicitation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise remains eligible, then the Servicer may continue to solicit the Borrower for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Flex Modification at its discretion, in accordance with the “continuation of
elements.label: text

elements.text: solicitation efforts” requirements in this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Continuation of solicitation efforts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must continue to try to contact and solicit the Borrower for alternatives to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure throughout the foreclosure process. After the 210th day after the Due Date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: an unpaid monthly installment, the Servicer has discretion to continue contact attempts
elements.label: text

elements.text: and must discontinue all contact attempts 60 days prior to a foreclosure sale date for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: judicial foreclosure or 30 days prior to a foreclosure sale date for a non-judicial
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure, unless the Servicer is required to continue contact attempts by applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Attempts to solicit for alternatives to foreclosure while the Mortgage is in foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be communicated to and coordinated with the foreclosure attorney, as appropriate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer must keep the attorney informed of the status of relevant alternative to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure negotiations and must notify the attorney within two Business Days after
elements.label: text

elements.text: arrangements for an alternative to foreclosure have been agreed to or within two Business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Days after the Mortgage is fully reinstated.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Borrower solicitation during bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer is not required to solicit a Borrower in an active Chapter 7 or Chapter 13
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy. However, a Borrower in an active Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy case
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be considered for an alternative to foreclosure if the Borrower, the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: counsel or bankruptcy trustee (with the Borrower’s written consent) submits a request to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Documents and forms
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following documents and forms have been developed to fulfill the solicitation
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Solicitation documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: In accordance with Section 9102.5(b) above, the Servicer must send, via regular or e￾mail, at least one Borrower Solicitation Package no later than the 45th day after the Due
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date of an unpaid monthly installment. The Borrower Solicitation Package consists of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following documents:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Exhibit 1145 (sent to Borrowers by the 45th after the Due Date of an unpaid monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: installment) and,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer may elect to send the following upon establishing quality right party
elements.label: text

elements.text: contact:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Form 710, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Frequently asked questions and foreclosure rescue scam notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 1145 is the solicitation template that reflects content a Servicer must include in its
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower Solicitation Package. In addition, Servicers must amend Exhibit 1145 as
elements.label: text

elements.text: necessary to comply with applicable law. The content of Exhibit 1145 includes:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A cover letter that includes a statement encouraging the Borrower to contact the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer, the Servicer’s contact information, a description of loss mitigation options
elements.label: text

elements.text: that are available and contact information for homeownership counseling
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information on available alternatives to foreclosure to help Borrowers understand the
elements.label: text

elements.text: options they may have to stay in their home or leave their home and avoid foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ FAQs to help answer Borrowers’ questions regarding the Borrower Response
elements.label: text

elements.text: Package and evaluation process. This information may also be provided on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s website.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Important notices, such as tips for avoiding foreclosure scams. This information may
elements.label: text

elements.text: also be provided on the Servicer’s website.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In accordance with Section 9102.5(b) above, the Servicer must send via regular or e-mail,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the applicable Freddie Mac Flex Modification solicitation cover letter and include the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Flex Modification Trial Period Plan Solicitation Offer – Not Based on an
elements.label: text

elements.text: Evaluation of a BRP to a Borrower in accordance with the requirements in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9206.1(c). Solicitation for the Freddie Mac Flex Modification is in addition to, and not in
elements.label: text

elements.text: place of, all other solicitation requirements described in this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The solicitation template included in Exhibits 1145, 1191, 1191A and 1191B may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: altered in the Servicer’s or attorney’s discretion as it deems necessary to meet the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of this section and to comply with disclosure and other requirements under
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Use of Form 710
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 710 is a required document that a Servicer must use to obtain Borrower and co￾Borrower financial information, including the cause/reasons for the Borrower or co￾Borrower’s financial hardship. The financial information and hardship sections of Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: 710 must be completed and the form executed by all Borrowers on the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must require the submission of Form 710 by all Borrowers requesting
elements.label: text

elements.text: consideration for an alternative to foreclosure. The Servicer may use a customized
elements.label: text

elements.text: equivalent of Form 710, provided that it requests the same financial information, hardship
elements.label: text

elements.text: affidavit and attestations from the Borrower. However, if the Servicer receives Form 710
elements.label: text

elements.text: rather than its customized equivalent, the Servicer must accept Form 710. References of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 710 in the Guide include any customized equivalent to Form 710.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: When sending the Borrower Solicitation Package, Servicers are authorized to edit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 710, if permitted by applicable law, to exclude references to Internal Revenue
elements.label: text

elements.text: Service (IRS) Form 4506-C; or, to the extent required by applicable law, to indicate that
elements.label: text

elements.text: such form may be required to complete the application and the circumstances when such
elements.label: text

elements.text: form must be obtained or processed in accordance with Section 9102.5(c)(ii).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Model letters
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must send the Borrower the appropriate Borrower Evaluation Notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: communicating the evaluation decision with one of the following outcomes:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reinstatement Notice*
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Repayment Plan Offer*
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Communications with the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Borrower Solicitation Package
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must provide in the cover letter included in the Borrower Solicitation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Package a specific date by which the package must be returned, which must be 30 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the date of the communication. In the event the 30th day falls on a non-Business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Day, the Servicer must specify the following Business Day as the date by which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower Response Package must be returned. Refer to Section 9102.5(c) for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation and content that must be included in a Borrower Solicitation Package.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Borrower Response Package
elements.label: text

elements.text: A complete Borrower Response Package must include the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Completed and signed Form 710
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Income documentation in accordance with Section 9202.1(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Hardship documentation in accordance with Section 9202.1(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Completed and signed IRS Form 4506-C, Request for Transcript of Tax Return, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: any of the following circumstances apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Servicer must submit IRS Form 4506-C, as applicable, to the IRS to obtain a
elements.label: text

elements.text: copy of the Borrower’s tax transcript. This is required:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ To reconcile inconsistencies between other information the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided (e.g., information the Borrower provided in Form 710) and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: income documentation; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When the Borrower is self-employed or is a fiscal year tax filer but the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower has not provided any of the required documentation specified in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9202.3(b) to support his or her income type
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ As otherwise requested by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may accept and rely on any information and documentation submitted on
elements.label: text

elements.text: behalf of a Borrower by the Borrower’s authorized trusted advisor, such as a HUD￾approved housing counselor, provided that the Servicer:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Complies with applicable privacy and other laws and, when necessary
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtains and maintains in the Mortgage file evidence of the Borrower’s written
elements.label: text

elements.text: consent to the Servicer’s sharing of the Borrower’s private financial and any other
elements.label: text

elements.text: protected information with the Borrower’s trusted advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer should notify Freddie Mac via an EDR transmission by reporting default
elements.label: text

elements.text: action code H5 within the first three Business Days of the month following the month
elements.label: text

elements.text: that it received a complete Borrower Response Package, but it is not required to do so.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Borrower is in an active Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy, the Servicer may:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accept copies of the bankruptcy schedules and tax returns (if returns are required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: be filed) in lieu of Form 710, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Use this information to determine Borrower eligibility (with the income
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers should request the schedules and tax returns from the Borrower, Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: counsel or bankruptcy court. If the bankruptcy schedules are greater than 90 days old as
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the date the Servicer first determined the Borrower submitted a complete Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Response Package, the Borrower must provide updated evidence of income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To the extent that a Servicer receives sufficient information to evaluate the Borrower for
elements.label: text

elements.text: a reinstatement or relief option in accordance with Chapter 9203 without the complete
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower Response Package, the Servicer should do so.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Acknowledgement of receipt of Borrower Response Package
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must acknowledge to the Borrower receipt of the Borrower Response
elements.label: text

elements.text: Package within five Business Days of receipt from the Borrower and must indicate
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether the package is complete or incomplete. The acknowledgement of receipt, which
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be in writing, must include the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer’s evaluation process and timeline
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An explanation of the foreclosure process, including that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Referral to foreclosure will not occur if the Servicer is reviewing a complete
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower Response Package
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Referral to foreclosure will not occur if the Servicer extends an offer for an
elements.label: text

elements.text: alternative to foreclosure and the Borrower’s deadline to respond has not expired
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Following referral to foreclosure, the foreclosure process may continue during the
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For a Borrower who submits a complete Borrower Response Package 37 days or less
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior to a scheduled foreclosure sale, an explanation of the Servicer’s plans for
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluating the Borrower for an alternative to foreclosure and suspending the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure, if appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A description of those situations where a court with jurisdiction over the foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeding (if any) or public official charged with carrying out the activity could fail
elements.label: text

elements.text: or refuse to halt a scheduled foreclosure sale, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any other disclosure as required under applicable law
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must maintain evidence of the date of receipt of the Borrower’s Response
elements.label: text

elements.text: Package in its records.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Incomplete information notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon receipt of an incomplete Borrower Response Package, the Servicer is required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: send an incomplete information notice to the Borrower at the same time as the
elements.label: text

elements.text: acknowledgement of receipt of the Borrower Response Package, which is no later than
elements.label: text

elements.text: five Business Days from receipt of the Borrower Response Package. The incomplete
elements.label: text

elements.text: information notice must include the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A list of documents or information needed to evaluate the Borrower for an alternative
elements.label: text

elements.text: to foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A reference to the HUD website for the following HUD-approved counseling
elements.label: text

elements.text: agencies as resources available to help the Borrower complete the package:
elements.label: text

elements.text:  The U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) at (800) 569-
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4287 or www.hud.gov/counseling
elements.label: text

elements.text:  The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) at (855) 411-2372 or
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://www.consumerfinance.gov/mortgagehelp/
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A reminder that if the Borrower does not submit all required documentation or
elements.label: text

elements.text: information, the Servicer may determine the Borrower to be ineligible for an
elements.label: text

elements.text: alternative to foreclosure and foreclosure proceedings will continue, including referral
elements.label: text

elements.text: to foreclosure if the Mortgage was not previously referred
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Statement indicating that, depending on when the necessary information or
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation is received, there is no guarantee that the Servicer may be able to
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluate the Borrower for alternatives to foreclosure and halt foreclosure proceedings
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A toll-free telephone number the Borrower may contact for questions on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: necessary documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within five Business Days of receiving the completed Borrower Response Package from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower, the Servicer is required to acknowledge to the Borrower that the completed
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower Response Package has been received.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer may, but is not required to, send an incomplete information notice to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower who submits an incomplete Borrower Response Package 37 days or less prior
elements.label: text

elements.text: to a scheduled foreclosure sale. The Servicer is strongly encouraged to work with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower who submits an incomplete Borrower Response Package 37 days or less prior
elements.label: text

elements.text: to a scheduled foreclosure sale to complete the Borrower Response Package and expedite
elements.label: text

elements.text: a decision.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notwithstanding receipt of an incomplete Borrower Response Package and the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: transmittal of an incomplete information notice, the Servicer must still refer a Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: to foreclosure while attempting to obtain a complete Borrower Response Package.

elements.text: (v) Evaluation requirements in response to a complete Borrower Response Package
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within five days of an evaluation decision but no later than 30 days following receipt of
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: following possible outcomes:

elements.text: ■ Offer reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment plan in accordance with the requirements of Chapter 9203.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Offer a modification as set forth in Chapter 9206 (i.e., Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Acceptance deadlines and foreclosure alternatives
elements.label: text

elements.text: For evaluation notices that are offers for alternatives to foreclosure, Servicers must
elements.label: text

elements.text: include in the evaluation notice a date by which the Borrowers must accept such
elements.label: text

elements.text: offers, which must be 14 days from the date of the evaluation notice for most
elements.label: text

elements.text: alternatives to foreclosure except a short sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must suspend foreclosure referral, the foreclosure process generally or
elements.label: text

elements.text: the foreclosure sale upon receiving a Borrower’s verbal (where permitted) or written
elements.label: text

elements.text: notification that he or she intends to accept an offer for an alternative to foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: other than a short sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Absent such written or verbal notification, the Servicer must suspend foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: referral, the foreclosure process, or the foreclosure sale upon receipt of a Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: first payment under a Trial Period Plan or other plan requiring payments (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment or forbearance plan). However, the repayment, forbearance or Trial Period
elements.label: text

elements.text: Plan will not be legally binding unless the first payment is made by no later than the
elements.label: text

elements.text: last day of the month that the first payment is due under the terms of the plan.

elements.text: (vi)Servicer evaluation obligations once foreclosure has been initiated
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Borrower Response Package received after referral to foreclosure and greater
elements.label: text

elements.text: than 37 days prior to the foreclosure sale date
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer receives a complete Borrower Response Package after referral to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure, the Servicer is not required to suspend foreclosure proceedings to review
elements.label: text

elements.text: the complete Borrower Response Package.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (I) Borrower Response Package received without a short sale purchase offer and
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale is scheduled
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the foreclosure sale has been scheduled and the Servicer sends the Borrower a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower Evaluation Notice that is an intent to pursue a short sale based on
elements.label: text

elements.text: receipt of a complete Borrower Response Package but a purchase offer was not
elements.label: text

elements.text: received, then the Borrower Evaluation Notice must be amended to indicate the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Contact a real estate broker to list your property for sale if it is not currently
elements.label: text

elements.text: listed. If your property is not currently listed, there may not be adequate time
elements.label: text

elements.text: to market the property or to review a potential buyer’s offer prior to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Once you receive a potential buyer’s offer, contact us immediately and we
elements.label: text

elements.text: will review the offer to determine whether to accept it in full satisfaction of
elements.label: text

elements.text: your obligation

elements.text: (II)Short sale evaluations
elements.label: text

elements.text: Once the Mortgage has been referred to foreclosure, the Servicer must continue
elements.label: text

elements.text: with foreclosure proceedings during the short sale process, except where delay for
elements.label: text

elements.text: motion of judgment is provided for Mortgages subject to judicial proceedings.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, the Servicer must not complete a foreclosure sale if the short sale has
elements.label: text

elements.text: been approved based on a review of a purchase offer and a complete Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Response Package (or other streamlined documentation permitted in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9208.1(c)). The Servicer must suspend the foreclosure sale where permitted under
elements.label: text

elements.text: State or local law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (III)Deed-in-lieu of foreclosure evaluations
elements.label: text

elements.text: Once the Mortgage has been referred to foreclosure, the Servicer must continue
elements.label: text

elements.text: with foreclosure proceedings during the deed-in-lieu of foreclosure process,
elements.label: text

elements.text: except where delay for motion of judgment is provided for Mortgages subject to
elements.label: text

elements.text: judicial proceedings. The Servicer may only suspend foreclosure sale once the
elements.label: text

elements.text: executed deed and all required deed-in-lieu of foreclosure documents have been
elements.label: text

elements.text: received by the Servicer. Additionally, the Servicer must receive an executed
elements.label: text

elements.text: deed from the Borrower no later than 30 days prior to the scheduled foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When considering a Borrower for a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensure that there is sufficient time to complete processing of the deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure (inclusive of sending the deed-in-lieu of foreclosure offer to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower) so that the Servicer receives the executed deed no later than 30 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior to the foreclosure sale date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Borrower Response Packages received 37-15 days prior to the scheduled
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale date
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a complete Borrower Response Package is received between 37 and 15 days,
elements.label: text

elements.text: inclusively, before a scheduled foreclosure sale date, the Servicer must expedite its
elements.label: text

elements.text: review and make a determination regarding the Borrower’s request for assistance at
elements.label: text

elements.text: least seven days before the scheduled foreclosure sale date. This will ensure that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer will be able to complete a foreclosure certification to the attorney seven days
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior to a scheduled foreclosure sale date, if appropriate, or offer the Borrower an
elements.label: text

elements.text: alternative to foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (C) Borrower Response Packages received less than 15 days prior to the scheduled
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale date
elements.label: text

elements.text: In cases where a complete Borrower Response Package is received less than 15 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior to a scheduled foreclosure sale date, the Servicer must first determine whether it
elements.label: text

elements.text: can conduct an expedited review of the Borrower Response Package and render a
elements.label: text

elements.text: decision by the foreclosure certification date (i.e., seven days prior to the scheduled
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale date) and if so, complete the review.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 9301.7(a) for additional requirements for Servicer evaluation obligations once
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure has been initiated.

elements.text: this section.

elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation required to be prepared, signed or sent by either the Servicer or the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: are considered “loss mitigation documents” for purposes of Section 1401.3(h).

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Evaluation Hierarchy and Performance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Standards
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9201.1: Rationale for Servicer loss mitigation activities
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s interest in a Mortgage, the Borrower and the home securing the Mortgage begins
elements.label: text

elements.text: when Freddie Mac purchases the Mortgage. The Borrower’s ownership of the home is
elements.label: text

elements.text: jeopardized when he or she stops making monthly payments. Freddie Mac is committed to
elements.label: text

elements.text: helping Borrowers find a solution to the Delinquency if they are experiencing financial difficulty
elements.label: text

elements.text: in making the Mortgage payment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower must make a commitment to reduce any discretionary, unnecessary expenditures
elements.label: text

elements.text: as a condition of Freddie Mac granting a relief or workout option. Additionally, the Borrower is
elements.label: text

elements.text: expected to work out similar relief or workout options with other creditors whenever possible.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac recognizes that not every Borrower who is experiencing financial difficulty can
elements.label: text

elements.text: retain home ownership. When this is the case, the Servicer can help a Borrower understand the
elements.label: text

elements.text: benefits of selling the property to avoid the consequences of foreclosure, where applicable, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the risk of a deficiency judgment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac wants the Servicer to pursue alternatives to foreclosure whenever possible because
elements.label: text

elements.text: they benefit not only the Borrower but also the Servicer, Freddie Mac and other interested parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Mortgage by:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eliminating the staff time and expense the Servicer incurs to service a delinquent Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: or a Mortgage in foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reinstating the Servicing fee income the Servicer earns if a Mortgage Delinquency is cured
elements.label: text

elements.text: or reinstating part or all of the Servicing fee income if a Mortgage is modified
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Improving the Servicer’s relationship with the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Minimizing Freddie Mac’s credit losses
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reducing an MI or guarantor’s claim payment, when applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Even after the Servicer has initiated foreclosure, it should still pursue alternatives to foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: to mitigate potential credit losses, whenever possible.
elements.label: text
