elements.text: evaluations in accordance with the standard loss mitigation evaluation hierarchy as described in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9201.2(a) or submit an exception request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower who was on a disaster-related forbearance completes the forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: without a solution to the delinquency, the Servicer must conduct reviews for proactive
elements.label: text

elements.text: Disaster Payment Deferral and proactive Freddie Mac Flex Modification offers in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with Sections 9203.4 (i)(vii), (i)(viii), (i)(ix), and (i)(x).
elements.label: text

elements.text: For the full requirements related to Servicing Mortgages impacted by a disaster, see Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Ineligible Borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower is not eligible for a relief or workout option but the Servicer believes that a
elements.label: text

elements.text: relief or workout option is still the best solution to the Delinquency, then the Servicer may
elements.label: text

elements.text: submit a recommendation to Freddie Mac for review along with the reason for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: recommendation, in accordance with the requirements in the relevant chapters for relief or
elements.label: text

elements.text: workout options.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9201.3: Servicer’s responsibilities for loss mitigation
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: activities (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer’s loss mitigation activities must include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pursuing reinstatement as the first option to resolve a Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Analyzing the Borrower’s financial situation and determining his or her intention toward the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage obligation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Determining the value and condition of the property to calculate the Borrower’s equity
elements.label: text

elements.text: position and Freddie Mac’s potential loss if Freddie Mac were to acquire the property as an
elements.label: text

elements.text: REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pursuing alternatives to foreclosure in accordance with the hierarchy set forth in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9201.2 when the Servicer determines the Borrower cannot reinstate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Continuing to pursue alternatives to foreclosure even after the Servicer has initiated
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure in accordance with Section 9102.4(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Managing the foreclosure process to ensure that expenses and accruing interest are
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimized and obtaining clear and marketable title as soon as legally possible
elements.label: text

elements.text: In conducting loss mitigation activities, the Servicer must also comply with all FHA, RHS, VA
elements.label: text

elements.text: or MI requirements, if applicable, and with the requirements in the table below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional requirements in conducting loss mitigation activities
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic Guide location
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquencies Chapter 9102
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reinstatements and relief options Chapter 9203
elements.label: text

elements.text: Hardest Hit Fund Chapter 9211
elements.label: text

elements.text: Foreclosure Chapter 9301
elements.label: text

elements.text: Bankruptcy Chapter 9401
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9201.4: Servicer’s loss mitigation operations
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Loss mitigation activities must be an integral part of the Servicer’s Servicing operations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac expects the Servicer’s collection and loss mitigation staff to coordinate their efforts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must ensure that it has:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. A documented process for loss mitigation activities and approving foreclosures
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Skilled staff devoted to loss mitigation activities
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Trained collections and loss mitigation staff who:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have the ability to gain a Borrower’s confidence and help the Borrower explore
elements.label: text

elements.text: alternatives to foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide counseling to a Borrower early in the Delinquency to explain potential options
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the consequences of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain a record of conversations with a Borrower in the Mortgage file or other form of
elements.label: text

elements.text: data storage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Screen Borrower requests for assistance to identify eligible workout candidates
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9201.5: Servicer default management performance
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: standards (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Call center performance standards
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Quality right party contact performance standard
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Alternative to foreclosure pull-through rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will measure a Servicer’s default management performance against certain metrics,
elements.label: text

elements.text: which may be amended by Freddie Mac from time to time.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer’s performance in the metrics will comprise its overall Servicer Success Scorecard
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment in the default management category of the Servicer Success Scorecard.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the performance standards prescribed below, a Servicer must also refer to its
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer Success Scorecard for the specific metrics and to Section 3501.2 for additional
elements.label: text

elements.text: information about the Servicer Success Scorecard.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Call center performance standards
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following terms are described for purposes of this section:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Call center definitions
elements.label: text

elements.text: A
elements.label: text

elements.text: Abandon Rate Percentage of calls made by the Servicer to Borrowers or by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower to a Servicer that are not intercepted by the Servicer (i.e., a
elements.label: text

elements.text: live operator) before the Borrower disconnects.
elements.label: text

elements.text: B
elements.label: text

elements.text: Blockage Rate Number of calls blocked, whether intentional or unintentional, as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: percentage of inbound calls directed to a Servicer’s call center.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: A Borrower attempting to call the Servicer receives a busy
elements.label: text

elements.text: signal as a result of a Servicer’s inbound call center’s failure to
elements.label: text

elements.text: receive such call.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers that maintain a call center must meet the following minimum call center and other
elements.label: text

elements.text: contact performance standards as measured in monthly increments:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Average speed to answer a telephone call of 60 seconds or less
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 5% or less telephone call Abandon Rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 1% or less telephone call Blockage Rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Average time for a live operator to respond to e-mails of 48 hours or less after receipt
elements.label: text

elements.text: (excluding automated responses)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Live operator chat response times of 5 minutes or less between chats
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers that maintain a call center must maintain data related to the performance standards
elements.label: text

elements.text: described above and must make such data available to Freddie Mac upon request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Quality right party contact performance standard
elements.label: text

elements.text: In its efforts to contact Borrowers, a Servicer must strive to achieve a quality right party
elements.label: text

elements.text: contact benchmark of at least 60% on its 120-day delinquent Freddie Mac portfolio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers in bankruptcy are excluded from the quality right party contact performance
elements.label: text

elements.text: standard. A Servicer’s obligation for establishing contact with a delinquent Borrower and
elements.label: text

elements.text: scope of a quality right party contact is set forth in Section 9102.3.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each time the Servicer achieves quality right party contact with a delinquent Borrower, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must notify Freddie Mac via an EDR transmission in accordance with the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ First quality right party contact: Servicers must report default action code AW and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: date of the first quality right party contact established with the Borrower within the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: three Business Days of the month following the month that the first quality right party
elements.label: text

elements.text: contact was established with the Borrower. Default action code AW is reported once.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Subsequent quality right party contact: Servicers must report default action code AX if
elements.label: text

elements.text: any subsequent quality right party contact was made with the Borrower after the initial
elements.label: text

elements.text: contact was established within the first three Business Days of the month following the
elements.label: text

elements.text: month that the subsequent contact occurred. Default action code AX may be reported in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the same month as default action code AW if a Servicer establishes a subsequent quality
elements.label: text

elements.text: right party contact with the Borrower in the same month that initial contact was achieved.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: A Servicer must continue to report default action code AX as often as it applies
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i.e., as often as the Servicer continues to establish quality right party contact with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower when working with the Borrower to resolve a Delinquency) with the date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: last quality right party contact for each month in which the action applies.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For additional information about EDR, refer to Section 9102.6 and Exhibit 82,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Default Reporting Transmission Code List.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Alternative to foreclosure pull-through rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers will be measured on their ability to successfully transition newly 60-day delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers into an alternative to foreclosure in a given quarter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: At a minimum, Servicers must achieve at least a 60% alternative to foreclosure pull-through
elements.label: text

elements.text: rate. The alternative to foreclosure pull-through rate is measured as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Alternatives to foreclosure in the current quarter divided by the prior quarter’s new 60-
elements.label: text

elements.text: day delinquent Borrower inflows
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reinstatements, forbearance plans, repayment plans, trial period plans, payoffs,
elements.label: text

elements.text: modifications, short sales and deeds-in-lieu of foreclosure are included in this
elements.label: text

elements.text: performance standard. Mortgages in bankruptcy are excluded from this measurement
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9201.6: Alternatives to foreclosure (09/10/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reinstatements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Relief options
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Workout options
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Charge-offs
elements.label: text

elements.text: Alternatives to foreclosure are reinstatements and relief and workout options that, when used,
elements.label: text

elements.text: result in a resolution of a Mortgage Delinquency without foreclosing on the property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Below is a list of the Freddie Mac alternatives to foreclosure,
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Reinstatements
elements.label: text

elements.text: A reinstatement restores a delinquent Mortgage to current status either with a lump-sum
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment of all amounts due or a partial payment followed by a repayment plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac has two types of reinstatements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Full reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Partial reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Chapter 9203 for additional information on reinstatements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Relief options
elements.label: text

elements.text: A relief option provides temporary relief to a Borrower and allows the Borrower to cure the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency over a defined period of time. Freddie Mac’s relief options are:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Chapter 9203 for additional information on relief options.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Workout options
elements.label: text

elements.text: A workout option is an alternative to foreclosure that eliminates or reduces the potential loss
elements.label: text

elements.text: that Freddie Mac would incur if the property securing the Mortgage were acquired through a
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Mortgage modification is a workout option that will enable the Borrower to retain
elements.label: text

elements.text: homeownership.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Chapter 9206 for additional information about modifications.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s workout options for situations where it is not feasible for the Borrower to
elements.label: text

elements.text: retain the property are:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Short sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Workout mortgage assumption
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional information about workout options
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic Guide location
elements.label: text

elements.text: Workout Mortgage assumption Chapter 9207
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Standard Short Sale Chapter 9208
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Standard Deed-in-Lieu of Foreclosure Chapter 9209
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Charge-offs
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac has a charge-off option available for use under certain conditions. A charge-off
elements.label: text

elements.text: ceases collection efforts on a Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 9210.1(b) for situations when Freddie Mac requires Servicers to make a
elements.label: text

elements.text: recommendation for a charge-off.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Delinquency, default and imminent danger of default
elements.label: text

elements.text: Throughout this chapter, Chapters 9203 through 9210 and 9301, Freddie Mac will use the
elements.label: text

elements.text: term Delinquency, which is defined in the Glossary, rather than the term default. Many of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: specific requirements for workout options are tied to the severity of the Delinquency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except as set forth in Sections 9206.1(e), 9208.1(c) and 9209.2, a Borrower is considered to
elements.label: text

elements.text: be in imminent danger of default, if, based upon the facts and circumstances of the particular
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage and using prudent business judgment, it appears more likely than not that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower will default on his or her Mortgage payments within the next 12 months.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Borrower must be delinquent in his or her Mortgage payments, or be in imminent danger
elements.label: text

elements.text: of default, to be considered for an alternative to foreclosure. The Borrower must document an
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible hardship to be eligible for certain workout and relief options.

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: As outlined in Section 5302.4, the following are acceptable alternatives for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s signature on the tax return(s):
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evidence the tax returns were filed electronically (e.g., signed Form 8879, IRS e-file
elements.label: text

elements.text: Signature Authorization or equivalent), or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Tax transcripts that validate the information on the unsigned tax returns, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A completed IRS Form 4506-C signed by the Borrower, or alternate form acceptable
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the IRS that authorizes the release of comparable tax information to a third party
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: All income must comply with all applicable federal, State and local laws,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ordinances, regulations, orders and regulatory guidance in accordance with Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Determining gross monthly income
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower’s gross income includes all taxable and non-taxable income, before any
elements.label: text

elements.text: payroll deductions, including, but not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Base wages and salaries, overtime pay, commissions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Fees, tips, bonuses, housing allowances
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other compensation for personal services, Social Security payments, including Social
elements.label: text

elements.text: Security received by adults on behalf of minors or by minors intended for their own
elements.label: text

elements.text: support
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Payments from annuities, insurance policies, retirement funds, and pensions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Disability or death benefits, and positive net rental income, except that the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: may, but is not required to, provide alimony, child support or separation maintenance
elements.label: text

elements.text: income
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must gross up all non-taxable income received by the Borrower only if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower is able to provide documentation verifying that the income is not taxable. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must maintain such documentation in the Mortgage file. To gross up non-taxable
elements.label: text

elements.text: income, the Servicer must multiply the amount of the non-taxable income by 1.25; if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: actual amount of federal or State taxes that would be paid is more than 25% of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s nontaxable income, the Servicer may use the actual percentage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must gross up all net income when the Borrower submits bank statements to
elements.label: text

elements.text: support the income type.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To gross up net income, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Establish the Borrower’s monthly net income in accordance with this Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Multiply the amount of the monthly net income by 1.25 to determine the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly gross income
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Exhibit 101, Income Calculation Guidelines for Alternative to Foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: Options, for additional instructions on calculating income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Substitution of income documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Borrower is in an active Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy, the Servicer may
elements.label: text

elements.text: accept copies of the bankruptcy schedules and tax returns (if returns are required to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: filed) in lieu of Form 710, and IRS Form 4506-C, and may use this information to
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine Borrower eligibility (with the income documentation). Servicers should
elements.label: text

elements.text: request the schedules and tax returns from the Borrower, Borrower’s counsel or
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy court. If the bankruptcy schedules are more than 90 days old as of the date
elements.label: text

elements.text: that such schedules are received by the Servicer, the Borrower must provide updated
elements.label: text

elements.text: evidence of income in accordance with this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Use of Third Party Vendors
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are reminded that per existing guidance around the use of outsourced vendors
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 8102.1(f), they may utilize Third Party Vendors for the verification of income
elements.label: text

elements.text: as outlined in Section 9202.1(c) above as long as they comply with all requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Analyze the Mortgage characteristics and terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must review the Mortgage characteristics and terms to determine what effect
elements.label: text

elements.text: they might have on determining a course of action to resolve the Delinquency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must compare the UPB and any delinquent amounts to the probable sale price
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the property to determine the Borrower’s equity position and Freddie Mac’s potential loss
elements.label: text

elements.text: should Freddie Mac have to acquire the property, if the Delinquency cannot be cured or
elements.label: text

elements.text: resolved.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must also review the Mortgage product type and the current interest rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: compared to current market interest rates to identify potential roadblocks in curing a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency. For example, if the Mortgage is an adjustable-rate Mortgage, the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be facing a new monthly payment that he or she cannot afford.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Analyze the reason for Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the course of performing collection activities, the Servicer should have determined why
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower has not remitted its Mortgage payments. Additionally, the Servicer should have
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined the Borrower’s willingness to resolve the Delinquency problem and have
elements.label: text

elements.text: counseled the Borrower about the consequences of the Delinquency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer has determined that the Borrower’s reason for not paying the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment is temporary, it may consider whether a partial reinstatement, a repayment plan or
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan may be a solution to cure the Delinquency. For the Servicer to consider
elements.label: text

elements.text: such alternatives, the Borrower must have the ability to make payments and bring the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage current within one year.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower’s financial situation and/or circumstances will not permit him or her to bring
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage current within a year, the Servicer must analyze the Borrower’s financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: situation and determine the Borrower’s desire to retain ownership of the home to determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: the appropriate solution to the Delinquency. See Section 9201.2 for the sequence in which a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower must be evaluated for loss mitigation solutions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Determine the best course of action for Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: After evaluating the Borrower’s financial information and circumstances, the value and
elements.label: text

elements.text: condition of the property, and the Mortgage characteristics and terms, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: begin to determine the best course of action to resolve the Delinquency. Based on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: information provided by the Borrower, the Servicer must determine which of the various
elements.label: text

elements.text: relief and workout options is appropriate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower’s property has been damaged by a natural or man-made disaster, or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower has an extraordinary circumstance, the Servicer should consider recommending a
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan. If the Borrower wants to keep the property and has a source of stable
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly income, the Servicer should consider whether to consider a repayment plan,
elements.label: text

elements.text: reinstatement or forbearance plan, or recommend a loan modification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower cannot or does not want to retain ownership of the property, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: should consider recommending workout options that allow the Borrower to sell or transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: the property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a relief or workout option will not resolve the Delinquency, the Servicer must initiate
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure in accordance with the requirements in Chapter 9301.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Chapter 8404 for additional requirements for Mortgages secured by properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: affected by a disaster.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9202.2: Mortgage and property risk analysis (09/10/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Service reconciliation of inconsistencies, fraud detection and document authenticity
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Risk of Property Ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Tenant-occupied properties built before 1978
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Service reconciliation of inconsistencies, fraud detection and document authenticity
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Reconciling income and/or hardship information inconsistencies
elements.label: text

elements.text: If, based on the Servicer’s good business judgment, the Borrower’s income and/or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: hardship information disclosed by the Borrower is inconsistent with the income and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: hardship documentation provided by the Borrower, the Servicer must attempt to reconcile
elements.label: text

elements.text: such inconsistencies. The Servicer must also document such material differences in its
elements.label: text

elements.text: servicing system.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Fraud detection
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not offer the Borrower a relief or workout option if there is reasonable
elements.label: text

elements.text: evidence indicating the Borrower submitted false or misleading information or otherwise
elements.label: text

elements.text: engaged in fraud in connection with the request for payment relief. A Servicer must use
elements.label: text

elements.text: good business judgment in determining whether the evidence is reasonably indicative of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s intentional misrepresentation or submission of false or misleading
elements.label: text

elements.text: information. Refer to Section 3201.2 for reporting requirements related to the discovery
elements.label: text

elements.text: of fraud or possible fraud on a Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Document authenticity
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer must use good business judgment when determining whether verification
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents are authentic and accurate, and allow the Servicer to underwrite the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: in accordance with the requirements of this Guide. A Servicer may elect to accept
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation with imperfections (blank fields, erasures, use of correction tape,
elements.label: text

elements.text: inaccurate dates, etc.) if the Servicer determines that the imperfections are immaterial to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the eligibility decision, are not indicative of fraud and do not impact the Servicer’s ability
elements.label: text

elements.text: to verify the completeness and accuracy of the Borrower’s financial representations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Risk of Property Ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: Risk of Property Ownership exists when there is a risk of liability to Freddie Mac if Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac becomes the owner of the property. For example, there is Risk of Property Ownership if
elements.label: text

elements.text: the condition of the Mortgaged Premises, or its immediate surroundings, pose a threat to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: health or safety of a property owner (e.g., condemnation), or there is otherwise a situation or
elements.label: text

elements.text: presence that is exigent or atypical that would cause a potential property owner to incur
elements.label: text

elements.text: extraordinary risk of liability if it becomes the owner of the property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must review the property inspection reports that the Servicer receives to
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine if a Risk of Property Ownership exists. Refer to Sections 8403.1(a) and 8403.1(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the requirements for managing a property securing a Mortgage identified as posing a Risk
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Property Ownership.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Tenant-occupied properties built before 1978
elements.label: text

elements.text: Before considering a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure on a tenant-occupied property built before
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1978, if the Servicer is aware of, or becomes aware of, any outstanding lead-based paint or
elements.label: text

elements.text: health code citations or violations against the property or property owner, it must notify
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac (see Directory 5), and include a copy of the citation or violation and any other
elements.label: text

elements.text: pertinent information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: After reviewing the documentation, Freddie Mac will provide the Servicer with instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the course of the action to take.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9202.3: Property inspections and abandonment
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ What is a property inspection?
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documenting property inspections
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When to order a property inspection
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Results of property inspection
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reimbursement of property inspections
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) What is a property inspection?
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except when an interior inspection is required (see Section 9202.3(c)(iii)), a property
elements.label: text

elements.text: inspection is an exterior inspection of the property to determine:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The condition of the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. If there is any waste, deterioration or vandalism
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The occupancy status
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. If the property has been abandoned
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. If the property is listed for sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. If there are obvious environmental hazards
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. If there is deferred maintenance or there are health and safety problems. The property is
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered to have deferred maintenance when the Borrower has allowed the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: condition to deteriorate to a point where general maintenance items such as trash
elements.label: text

elements.text: removal, painting, yard work and general repairs have not been performed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. If visible asset preservation is needed
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Documenting property inspections
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Form to use
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer must document each inspection of the property with an inspection report that
elements.label: text

elements.text: is prepared on Form 1013, 1-4 Unit Property Inspection Report, or a substitute report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The substitute report can be the Servicer’s proprietary form or a form the inspection
elements.label: text

elements.text: company performing the inspection prepares, including a computer-generated or
elements.label: text

elements.text: automated form. The substitute form must contain all of the data elements on Form 1013.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Retaining inspection reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must retain the inspection reports in the Mortgage file or other form of data
elements.label: text

elements.text: storage for the same period of time that it retains the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) When to order a property inspection
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgaged Premises must be inspected in accordance with the requirements below to
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine the physical condition and occupancy status.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may use a third party to conduct property inspections.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Inspection dates
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must order a property inspection on or after the 90th day of Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: and obtain the complete inspection report by the 120th day of Delinquency. The Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must continue to obtain a newly completed property inspection report every calendar
elements.label: text

elements.text: month. No two reports may be completed within a 20-day period while the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: remains 90 days or more delinquent. However, weekly and biweekly ordinance-required
elements.label: text

elements.text: property inspections (reimbursable under expense codes 404016 and 404017) are exempt
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the 20-day limitation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Discontinuance of property inspections
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must discontinue obtaining newly-completed property inspection reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: when or upon:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer has achieved quality right party contact within the last 30 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower has made a full P&I payment within the last 30 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower has been approved for, or is performing under the terms of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: reinstatement, relief option, or workout option
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower has filed for bankruptcy and is performing under the applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy plan, or where otherwise performing a property inspection would be
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered a violation of the automatic stay provision
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer has successfully reported the foreclosure sale date or the deed-in-lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure via Resolve® pursuant to Section 9603.1(a); or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The property is sold to a third party at the foreclosure sale and the foreclosure action
elements.label: text

elements.text: is complete. (Note that, depending upon the jurisdiction, a foreclosure action may not
elements.label: text

elements.text: be complete until the sale is confirmed or ratified.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the property is found to be vacant or if the occupancy is unknown, property inspections
elements.label: text

elements.text: must continue as long as the Mortgage remains 90 or more days delinquent regardless of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer achieving quality right party contact or a prospective resolution to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event that extenuating circumstances cause property inspection delays, Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: are instructed to document the mortgage file explaining the cause of the delays.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Curbside inspections
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac does not generally consider a curbside or “drive-by” property inspection to
elements.label: text

elements.text: be a sufficient exterior property inspection. However, Freddie Mac will permit a curbside
elements.label: text

elements.text: inspection to be performed in the following circumstances:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Legal constraints due to compliance with federal, State or local law, including, but
elements.label: text

elements.text: not limited to, an active bankruptcy by the Borrower; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Personal danger to the inspector
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Abandonment or waste
elements.label: text

elements.text: An abandoned property is:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. A property to which the owner has voluntarily and intentionally relinquished
elements.label: text

elements.text: ownership, claim and control, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. As otherwise defined under local laws. Factors evidencing abandonment include
elements.label: text

elements.text: vacancy, waste, deterioration and lack of utilities.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Servicer learns that the Mortgaged Premises have been abandoned or are in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: severe state of deterioration, waste or disrepair that warrants a physical condition rating
elements.label: text

elements.text: of “poor” on Form 1013, 1-4 Unit Property Inspection Report, the Servicer must take
elements.label: text

elements.text: action as required in Section 8403.1 including, but not limited to, ordering a monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: property inspection, even if the Mortgage payments are less than 60 days delinquent.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, if the Mortgaged Premises have been confirmed as abandoned, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must obtain monthly interior inspections, in accordance with the terms of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents and applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Property seizure
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to the following Sections for property inspection requirements related to property
elements.label: text

elements.text: seizure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional guidelines related to property seizures
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic Guide location
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unsolicited disclosure of pre-seizure information Section 8402.1(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Requested disclosure of pre-seizure information Section 8402.1(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Government notice of property seizure to the Servicer Section 8402.2(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Results of property inspection
elements.label: text

elements.text: Review the completed inspection report and determine if any further action is needed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Results of property inspection
elements.label: text

elements.text: IF the property inspection indicated
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the property is...
elements.label: text

elements.text: THEN the Servicer must...
elements.label: text

elements.text: Listed for sale ■ Obtain the name and telephone number of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the listing broker and retain for future use
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Enter in its monthly EDR, the date that it
elements.label: text

elements.text: became aware that the property was listed for
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: Vacant and/or not maintained Follow the requirements in Section 8403.1
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Reimbursement of property inspections
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each property inspection completed in accordance with the requirements set forth in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9202.3(c), Freddie Mac will reimburse the Servicer the lesser of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The actual cost of the property inspection
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9202.4: When to obtain a property value (12/17/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When to obtain a property value
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtaining a property value and Borrower Disclosures
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Checking the status of orders for property valuations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Valuation costs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Remitting BPO cost reimbursements to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) When to obtain a property value
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Obtaining a property value in connection with an alternative to foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must obtain a property value from Freddie Mac when considering a Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: for a workout. The requirements for obtaining a property value differ based on the type of
elements.label: text

elements.text: alternative to foreclosure for which the Borrower is being considered. Refer to Chapters
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9206 through 9210 for the requirements for obtaining a property value for each specific
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure alternative.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the Mortgage is covered by mortgage insurance, the Servicer must ensure that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the property value it obtains is based on a property valuation type that is consistent with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the MI’s requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Obtaining a property value to prepare foreclosure sale bidding instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are not required to obtain property values when preparing foreclosure sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: bidding instructions on properties subject to resale restrictions that survive foreclosure or
elements.label: text

elements.text: recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure (in accordance with Chapters 4406, 4502 or
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4504, as applicable). The Servicer must refer to the applicable resale restrictions for
elements.label: text

elements.text: details on the property valuation and/or the restricted resale price, as well as any other
elements.label: text

elements.text: terms and conditions that must be met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If State law requires that an appraisal report be used to set the bid, then the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtain the appraisal report in sufficient time to complete the foreclosure by the scheduled
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale date. The Servicer may obtain the appraisal report either directly from
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac through BPOdirect® or from an appraiser of its choice.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicer does not obtain the appraisal report directly from Freddie Mac, then the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtain Freddie Mac’s prior written approval to order the appraisal report from an
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraiser of its choice at least 30 days before the scheduled foreclosure sale date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s approval can be obtained by submitting a request for pre-approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: (RPA) via PAID (Payments Automated Intelligent and Dynamic) (see Exhibit 88,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Tools). If a Servicer does not obtain Freddie Mac’s prior approval, Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac will not reimburse the Servicer for the appraisal expense.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure the appraisal is obtained in accordance with applicable law, and the appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: must comply with the requirements of Topic 5600 and Section 9301.8(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: When State law does not require an appraisal report to be used to set the bid, unless the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale is of a property subject to resale restrictions that survive foreclosure or
elements.label: text

elements.text: recordation of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, the Servicer must comply with the credit bid
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Section 9301.8(b) for First-Lien Mortgages not covered by mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In States where the sheriff orders an appraisal to determine the opening bid at a
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure sale, the Servicer must still determine the maximum bid the Servicer is
elements.label: text

elements.text: authorized to make on Freddie Mac’s behalf. (See Sections 9301.8(a) through 9301.8(f)
elements.label: text

elements.text: for instructions for bidding at a foreclosure sale.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Obtaining a property value and Borrower Disclosures
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regardless of whether the Servicer, Freddie Mac or both are evaluating the Borrower for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: workout or relief option, the Servicer must provide the Borrower such notices and valuations
elements.label: text

elements.text: as are required by applicable law, including the Equal Credit Opportunity Act. This includes
elements.label: text

elements.text: notices and valuations developed in connection with a workout or relief option that is
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered to be an application for credit. The Servicer must give the Borrower the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: valuation provided to the Servicer by Freddie Mac through BPOdirect or other sources.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac participated in evaluating a Borrower for a workout or relief option that gives
elements.label: text

elements.text: rise to an obligation to provide the Borrower a valuation developed in connection with an
elements.label: text

elements.text: application for a workout or relief option, then the Servicer must provide the valuation to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower on behalf of Freddie Mac. In these cases, the communication from the Servicer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower transmitting the valuation must identify both the Servicer and Freddie Mac as
elements.label: text

elements.text: providing the valuation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Obtaining a property value via BPOdirect
elements.label: text

elements.text: For requirements on obtaining a property value via BPOdirect, refer to Section 2406.1.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 9209.5(d) for additional details on obtaining a property valuation for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: deed-in-lieu of foreclosure. Refer to Section 9210.1(c) for valuation requirements to
elements.label: text

elements.text: submit a recommendation for a charge-off.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Property values obtained via the Freddie Mac Real Estate Valuation and Pricing
elements.label: text

elements.text: tool
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except for Mortgages secured by properties subject to resale restrictions (in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with Chapter 4502 or 4406, as applicable), the Servicer must obtain a property value
elements.label: text

elements.text: through the Real Estate Valuation and Pricing tool (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools) for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower being considered for a Standard Short Sale. Refer to Section 9208.2(a) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional details on obtaining a short sale property value.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Based on the information provided by the Servicer, Resolve® will determine the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property value of each Mortgage under consideration for a Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification®.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Checking the status of orders for property valuations
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer may determine the status of its BPO order by accessing BPODirect and entering
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Freddie Mac loan number. Contact Freddie Mac with any questions about a BPO order
elements.label: text

elements.text: (see Directory 6).
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer may determine the status of the property valuation ordered in the Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Real Estate Valuation and Pricing tool (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools) for the purposes of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Standard Short Sale pursuant to Section 9202.4(b). (See also Section 9208.2(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: for details on obtaining a short sale property value via the Freddie Mac Real Estate Valuation
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Pricing tool.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Valuation costs
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac does not charge a fee for automated valuations. For all other valuations, the cost
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the valuation depends on whether an interior or exterior valuation is conducted. Servicers

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 9203: Reinstatements and Relief
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Options
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9203.1: Reinstatements and relief options (04/27/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2026-2, which announced updates related to Resolve® reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for repayment plans and forbearance agreements. Servicers may implement
elements.label: text

elements.text: the new requirements prior to the mandatory October 1, 2026 version of this section if they
elements.label: text

elements.text: are operationally ready to do so.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reinstatements and relief options
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General requirements for reinstatements and relief options
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Types of reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When to accept full reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When to accept partial reinstatement of a Mortgage in foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reporting and processing the reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reimbursement of expenses related to reinstatements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Reinstatements and relief options
elements.label: text

elements.text: The most desirable resolution to a Delinquency is reinstatement. Reinstatement is the act of
elements.label: text

elements.text: restoring a delinquent Mortgage to current status. When the Servicer’s collection efforts do
elements.label: text

elements.text: not result in reinstatement of a delinquent Mortgage, the Servicer should consider offering
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower a relief option.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A relief option is an alternative to foreclosure that provides a Borrower with temporary relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: or an opportunity to cure a Delinquency over a defined period of time or bring the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency immediately current.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer has a Borrower whose situation does not meet Freddie Mac’s relief option
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility requirements but the Servicer feels a relief option is still the best possible solution
elements.label: text

elements.text: to cure the Delinquency, the Servicer must submit a request to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5)
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Freddie Mac’s review. The Servicer must use Form 105, Multipurpose Loan Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transmittal, and explain why a relief option is the best solution to the Delinquency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In lieu of having the Servicer or Borrower prepare, sign and send, return or submit paper
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents necessary to process a reinstatement or relief option, the Servicer and Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: may provide loss mitigation information and documents electronically.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 1401.3(h) for eligible documents and related requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) General requirements for reinstatements and relief options
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all reinstatements and relief options, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Comply with the requirements of the FHA, VA, RHS or MI and obtain approval from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: FHA, VA, RHS or MI, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Retain any written agreements in the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Within two Business Days of the date on which the Servicer receives a written request for
elements.label: text

elements.text: payoff or reinstatement figures, provide complete, written figures as of the request date to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the attorney, trustee, workout specialist or outsourcing vendor requesting the information
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all reinstatements and relief options, the Servicer must not:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Demand payment of expenses from the Borrower in excess of the costs the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: actually incurred at the time the Borrower reinstates the Mortgage. These costs may not
elements.label: text

elements.text: exceed Freddie Mac’s approved expense amounts (see Exhibit 57A, Approved Attorney,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Foreclosure, Mediation, Postponement Fees and Title Expenses) unless the Servicer has
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtained Freddie Mac’s approval to exceed these amounts, which approval may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: requested by submitting Form 105 to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) prior to incurring
elements.label: text

elements.text: the expense.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Refuse to reinstate a Mortgage if the Borrower is unwilling or unable to pay a
elements.label: text

elements.text: reinstatement or relief fee that is allowed by applicable law or the Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Among available options, the Servicer may defer collection of the fee until payoff of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Charge the Borrower any of the costs itemized in Section 9701.4(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Types of reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: There are two types of reinstatement: full and partial.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Full reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: A full reinstatement occurs when the Borrower restores a delinquent Mortgage to current
elements.label: text

elements.text: status by paying the total delinquent amount, including advances, delinquent principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: and interest, legal costs and other expenses incurred and that are past due under the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the Security Instrument.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not charge or collect from the Borrower an amount that exceeds the
elements.label: text

elements.text: expense limits specified in Exhibit 57A, except in cases where a higher expense amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: is legally allowable and recoverable. In addition, the payment must not include
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement for costs the Servicer incurred if applicable law prohibits collection of
elements.label: text

elements.text: those costs from the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Partial reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: A partial reinstatement occurs when the Borrower makes at least one full contractual
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly payment on a delinquent Mortgage but does not bring the Mortgage current.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must report the loan level transaction in accordance with Section 8303.3(j).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Partial reinstatement and repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: A partial reinstatement should be coupled with a repayment plan to restore a delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage to current status, but doing so is not mandatory. When processing a repayment
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower must pay an amount that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Equals or exceeds at least one full contractual monthly payment on a delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Is less than the total delinquent amount due, including delinquent principal and
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest, advances, legal costs and other expenses incurred and that are past due
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the Note and the Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower must agree to a repayment plan that provides for payment of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: remaining arrearage, includes a stipulation that suspends the foreclosure proceedings
elements.label: text

elements.text: during the repayment plan and allows the Servicer to recommence foreclosure at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: point the foreclosure was suspended if the Borrower defaults on the repayment plan,
elements.label: text

elements.text: unless otherwise prohibited by applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Exhibit 93, Evaluation Notices, includes a model repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: template and a model reinstatement template that the Servicer may combine
elements.label: text

elements.text: and amend as necessary to comply with the requirements of this section and
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must not charge or collect from the Borrower an amount that exceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: the expense limits specified in Exhibit 57, 1- to 4-Unit Property Approved Expense
elements.label: text

elements.text: Amounts, and Exhibit 57A unless those amounts were approved by Freddie Mac. In
elements.label: text

elements.text: addition, the payment must not include reimbursement for costs the Servicer incurred
elements.label: text

elements.text: if applicable law prohibits collection of those costs from the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers that choose not to pair a partial reinstatement with a repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: must continue to attempt to resolve the Delinquency and remain responsible for
elements.label: text

elements.text: adhering to foreclosure timeline requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) When to accept full reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must accept full reinstatement of a Mortgage if the payment, in cash or certified
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds, includes the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Total delinquent amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accrued interest and unpaid principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Actual legal costs, including any advances and additional expenses incurred
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Costs for BPO, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Inspection fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accrued late charges.
elements.label: text

elements.text: With the exception of inspection fees, regardless of expenses incurred, the Servicer must not
elements.label: text

elements.text: charge the Borrower an amount that exceeds the expense limits specified in Exhibit 57A. In
elements.label: text

elements.text: addition, the payment must not include reimbursement for costs the Servicer incurred if State
elements.label: text

elements.text: law prohibits collection of those costs from the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not refuse to accept a full reinstatement if the Borrower offers to pay all
elements.label: text

elements.text: amounts due except for the cost of the BPO, inspection fees and accrued late charges. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may make arrangements with the Borrower to pay the late charges after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is reinstated. If the Borrower can pay all arrearages, inspection fees and costs,
elements.label: text

elements.text: except for the cost of the BPO, the Servicer is not required to collect the BPO cost from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower or remit the cost of the BPO to Freddie Mac. The Servicer must document the
elements.label: text

elements.text: reason for nonremittance of the BPO cost in the file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must collect the cost of the BPO before it can collect any accrued late charges.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) When to accept partial reinstatement of a Mortgage in foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must accept partial reinstatement of a Mortgage referred to foreclosure if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following conditions are met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Borrower submits payment in cash or certified funds of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All outstanding legal fees and related expenses, in amounts not to exceed the expense
elements.label: text

elements.text: limits specified in Exhibit 57A, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An amount equal to, at a minimum, the first payment due under the repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. If there is an Escrow account on the Mortgage, the Borrower must enter into a repayment
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan with the Servicer that includes an agreement to repay the remaining delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest, principal and Escrow (if applicable) and the scheduled monthly payments, no
elements.label: text

elements.text: later than 12 months from the date the Servicer receives the partial reinstatement funds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The repayment plan must be in writing if the plan exceeds three months. If the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: was referred to foreclosure prior to extending the repayment plan, the Borrower must
elements.label: text

elements.text: make the first payment under the agreement in order to accept the terms of the plan. If
elements.label: text

elements.text: there is no Escrow account on the Mortgage, the Servicer must establish one if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment period exceeds three months (unless prohibited by applicable law) and then
elements.label: text

elements.text: may extend the repayment period to no more than 18 months without Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The plan must state:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The amount of the partial reinstatement payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The total amount to be repaid
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Beginning and end dates of the repayment period
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The exact amount of the repayment plan payment that will be added to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s regular monthly payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That the Borrower must pay all scheduled monthly payments during the period of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That the Borrower must continue monthly payments according to the terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note at the end of the plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower agrees that the foreclosure proceedings are suspended during the
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment plan and that the Servicer is entitled to recommence foreclosure at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: point the foreclosure was suspended if the Borrower defaults on the repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 93, includes a model repayment plan template and model reinstatement template
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the Servicer may combine and amend as necessary to comply with the requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: of this section and applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The Borrower must submit information demonstrating his or her financial ability to make
elements.label: text

elements.text: the:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Partial reinstatement payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Repayment plan payments (including the scheduled monthly payment) on a monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: basis
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monthly payments at the end of the repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: At the end of the repayment plan, the Servicer may make arrangements with the Borrower to
elements.label: text

elements.text: collect accrued late charges, inspection fees and the cost of the BPO. However, the payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: must not include reimbursement for costs the Servicer incurred if State law prohibits
elements.label: text

elements.text: collection of those costs from the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer or the Borrower propose a partial reinstatement plan that does not meet
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s criteria, but the Servicer believes it will result in the Borrower’s reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Mortgage, the Servicer must submit the plan on Form 105 to Freddie Mac (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Directory 5) for consideration.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Reporting and processing the reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Processing partial reinstatements and repayment plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must also notify Freddie Mac via an EDR transmission within three
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Days of the month following the month in which it accepted the initial payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: on a repayment plan or a partial reinstatement of a Mortgage that was reported in
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure in the previous month and needs to be moved to delinquent status from
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure status. The Servicer must include the following activity in the EDR
elements.label: text

elements.text: transmission:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Default action code 12 (Repayment Plan) indicates a repayment plan once the initial
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment plan payment is received. The Servicer must continue reporting this code
elements.label: text

elements.text: for each month of the repayment period except for the last month of the repayment
elements.label: text

elements.text: period in which the Mortgage will be fully reinstated.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Default action code 20 (Reinstatement (Partial)) is only required if, as a result of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: partial reinstatement without a repayment plan, the Servicer needs to change the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage status from foreclosure to delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Servicers should not report default action code 12 in the last month of the repayment
elements.label: text

elements.text: period
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must continue to report the default action code 12 (Repayment Plan) for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage in its future monthly EDR transmissions, provided the Borrower is in
elements.label: text

elements.text: compliance with the terms of the repayment plan, until the Mortgage has been fully
elements.label: text

elements.text: reinstated (see above steps one through three) or paid off or a workout has been
elements.label: text

elements.text: completed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower fails to make a payment due under the repayment plan, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: discontinue reporting default action code 12 on its EDR transmission.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For additional information about EDR, refer to Section 9102.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage has been inactivated by Freddie Mac, the Servicer must report partial
elements.label: text

elements.text: reinstatement in the Loan-Level Transaction as required in Section 8303.3(j).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Processing requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer accepts a full or partial reinstatement on a Mortgage that is or was in
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure, then it must process the transaction as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Take all necessary actions to prevent additional foreclosure expenses from being
elements.label: text

elements.text: incurred, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Pay any foreclosure expenses that were actually incurred
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Apply any remaining funds to the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Return the Note, if appropriate, with a paper copy of the original or electronic or
elements.label: text

elements.text: system-generated version of Form 1036, Request for Physical or Constructive
elements.label: text

elements.text: Possession of Documents, to the Document Custodian by certified mail.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If one of Freddie Mac’s Designated Custodians is holding the Note, submit the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: and any additional documentation, if required, as directed by the Designated
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers that use The Bank of New York Mellon Trust Company, N.A., as
elements.label: text

elements.text: Designated Custodian, and use its web-based process will include a copy of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: electronically generated 1036 Release Receipt Report when returning such items to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Designated Custodian (see Section 8107.1).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Additional requirements for Mortgages with buydown funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional requirements for Mortgages with buydown funds are as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When processing a reinstatement for a Mortgage subject to a temporary subsidy
elements.label: text

elements.text: buydown plan in accordance with Section 4204.3, the Servicer must not apply funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: in a buydown account to bring a delinquent Mortgage current unless it is required per
elements.label: text

elements.text: the terms of the buydown agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Upon reinstatement of a Mortgage that is subject to a temporary subsidy buydown
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan, the Borrower must resume their monthly payment amount as specified in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: terms of the buydown agreement, subject to applicable law and the terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: buydown agreement*
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the buydown term has expired or if, for any reason, the buydown funds are not
elements.label: text

elements.text: available or the buydown funds are not paid, the Borrower is responsible to continue
elements.label: text

elements.text: to make full monthly Mortgage payments as required by the terms of the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any remaining funds held in association with a buydown account, upon the end of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: buydown term or the liquidation of the Mortgage, whichever occurs first, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must distribute in accordance with applicable law and per the buydown agreement or
elements.label: text

elements.text: apply to the Mortgage consistent with Section 4204.3(e)
elements.label: text

elements.text: *Application of each monthly payment must continue to be applied in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the terms of the buydown agreement, applicable law and the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Reimbursement of expenses related to reinstatements
elements.label: text

elements.text: If applicable federal, State or local law prohibits collection of incurred expenses (e.g., legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: fees and/or legal costs) from the Borrower upon reinstatement of the Mortgage, Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: will reimburse certain expenses the Servicer incurs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 9701.1(e)(iv) regarding unrecoverable expenses.
