elements.text: 9203.1: Reinstatements and relief options (Future
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: effective date 10/01/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reinstatements and relief options
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General requirements for reinstatements and relief options
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Types of reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When to accept full reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When to accept partial reinstatement of a Mortgage in foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reporting and processing the reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reimbursement of expenses related to reinstatements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Reinstatements and relief options
elements.label: text

elements.text: The most desirable resolution to a Delinquency is reinstatement. Reinstatement is the act of
elements.label: text

elements.text: restoring a delinquent Mortgage to current status. When the Servicer’s collection efforts do
elements.label: text

elements.text: not result in reinstatement of a delinquent Mortgage, the Servicer should consider offering
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower a relief option.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A relief option is an alternative to foreclosure that provides a Borrower with temporary relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: or an opportunity to cure a Delinquency over a defined period of time or bring the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency immediately current.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The most desirable resolution to a Delinquency is a reinstatement. When the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: collection efforts do not result in reinstatement of a delinquent Mortgage, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluate Borrowers in accordance with the Freddie Mac loss mitigation evaluation hierarchy
elements.label: text

elements.text: as set forth in Section 9201.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer has a Borrower whose situation does not meet Freddie Mac’s relief option
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility requirements but the Servicer feels a relief option is still the best possible solution
elements.label: text

elements.text: to cure the Delinquency, the Servicer must submit a request to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5)
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Freddie Mac’s review. The Servicer must use Form 105, Multipurpose Loan Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transmittal, and explain why a relief option is the best solution to the Delinquency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In lieu of having the Servicer or Borrower prepare, sign and send, return or submit paper
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents necessary to process a reinstatement or relief option, the Servicer and Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: may provide loss mitigation information and documents electronically.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 1401.3(h) for eligible documents and related requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) General requirements for reinstatements and relief options
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all reinstatements and relief options, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Comply with the requirements of the FHA, VA, RHS or MI and obtain approval from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: FHA, VA, RHS or MI, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Retain any written agreements in the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Within two Business Days of the date on which the Servicer receives a written request for
elements.label: text

elements.text: payoff or reinstatement figures, provide complete, written figures as of the request date to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the attorney, trustee, workout specialist or outsourcing vendor requesting the information
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all reinstatements and relief options, the Servicer must not:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Demand payment of expenses from the Borrower in excess of the costs the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: actually incurred at the time the Borrower reinstates the Mortgage. These costs may not
elements.label: text

elements.text: exceed Freddie Mac’s approved expense amounts (see Exhibit 57A, Approved Attorney,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Foreclosure, Mediation, Postponement Fees and Title Expenses) unless the Servicer has
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtained Freddie Mac’s approval to exceed these amounts, which approval may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: requested by submitting Form 105 to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) prior to incurring
elements.label: text

elements.text: the expense.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Refuse to reinstate a Mortgage if the Borrower is unwilling or unable to pay a
elements.label: text

elements.text: reinstatement or relief fee that is allowed by applicable law or the Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Among available options, the Servicer may defer collection of the fee until payoff of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Charge the Borrower any of the costs itemized in Section 9701.4(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Types of reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: There are two types of reinstatement: full and partial.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Full reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: A full reinstatement occurs when the Borrower restores a delinquent Mortgage to current
elements.label: text

elements.text: status by paying the total delinquent amount, including advances, delinquent principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: and interest, legal costs and other expenses incurred and that are past due under the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the Security Instrument.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not charge or collect from the Borrower an amount that exceeds the
elements.label: text

elements.text: expense limits specified in Exhibit 57A, except in cases where a higher expense amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: is legally allowable and recoverable. In addition, the payment must not include
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimbursement for costs the Servicer incurred if applicable law prohibits collection of
elements.label: text

elements.text: those costs from the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Partial reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: A partial reinstatement occurs when the Borrower makes at least one full contractual
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly payment on a delinquent Mortgage but does not bring the Mortgage current.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must report the loan level transaction in accordance with Section 8303.3(j).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Partial reinstatement and repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: A partial reinstatement should be coupled with a repayment plan to restore a delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage to current status, but doing so is not mandatory. When processing a repayment
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower must pay an amount that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Equals or exceeds at least one full contractual monthly payment on a delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Is less than the total delinquent amount due, including delinquent principal and
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest, advances, legal costs and other expenses incurred and that are past due
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the Note and the Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower must agree to a repayment plan that provides for payment of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: remaining arrearage, includes a stipulation that suspends the foreclosure proceedings
elements.label: text

elements.text: during the repayment plan and allows the Servicer to recommence foreclosure at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: point the foreclosure was suspended if the Borrower defaults on the repayment plan,
elements.label: text

elements.text: unless otherwise prohibited by applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Exhibit 93, Evaluation Notices, includes a model repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: template and a model reinstatement template that the Servicer may combine
elements.label: text

elements.text: and amend as necessary to comply with the requirements of this section and
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must not charge or collect from the Borrower an amount that exceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: the expense limits specified in Exhibit 57, 1- to 4-Unit Property Approved Expense
elements.label: text

elements.text: Amounts, and Exhibit 57A unless those amounts were approved by Freddie Mac. In
elements.label: text

elements.text: addition, the payment must not include reimbursement for costs the Servicer incurred
elements.label: text

elements.text: if applicable law prohibits collection of those costs from the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers that choose not to pair a partial reinstatement with a repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: must continue to attempt to resolve the Delinquency and remain responsible for
elements.label: text

elements.text: adhering to foreclosure timeline requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) When to accept full reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must accept full reinstatement of a Mortgage if the payment, in cash or certified
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds, includes the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Total delinquent amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accrued interest and unpaid principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Actual legal costs, including any advances and additional expenses incurred
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Costs for BPO, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Inspection fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accrued late charges.
elements.label: text

elements.text: With the exception of inspection fees, regardless of expenses incurred, the Servicer must not
elements.label: text

elements.text: charge the Borrower an amount that exceeds the expense limits specified in Exhibit 57A. In
elements.label: text

elements.text: addition, the payment must not include reimbursement for costs the Servicer incurred if State
elements.label: text

elements.text: law prohibits collection of those costs from the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not refuse to accept a full reinstatement if the Borrower offers to pay all
elements.label: text

elements.text: amounts due except for the cost of the BPO, inspection fees and accrued late charges. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may make arrangements with the Borrower to pay the late charges after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is reinstated. If the Borrower can pay all arrearages, inspection fees and costs,
elements.label: text

elements.text: except for the cost of the BPO, the Servicer is not required to collect the BPO cost from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower or remit the cost of the BPO to Freddie Mac. The Servicer must document the
elements.label: text

elements.text: reason for nonremittance of the BPO cost in the file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must collect the cost of the BPO before it can collect any accrued late charges.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) When to accept partial reinstatement of a Mortgage in foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must accept partial reinstatement of a Mortgage referred to foreclosure if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following conditions are met.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All outstanding legal fees and related expenses, in amounts not to exceed the expense
elements.label: text

elements.text: limits specified in Exhibit 57A, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An amount equal to, at a minimum, the first payment due under the repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. If there is an Escrow account on the Mortgage, the Borrower must enter into a repayment
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan with the Servicer that includes an agreement to repay the remaining delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest, principal and Escrow (if applicable) and the scheduled monthly payments, no
elements.label: text

elements.text: later than 12 months from the date the Servicer receives the partial reinstatement funds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The repayment plan must be in writing if the plan exceeds three months. If the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: was referred to foreclosure prior to extending the repayment plan, the Borrower must
elements.label: text

elements.text: make the first payment under the agreement in order to accept the terms of the plan. If
elements.label: text

elements.text: there is no Escrow account on the Mortgage, the Servicer must establish one if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment period exceeds three months (unless prohibited by applicable law) and then
elements.label: text

elements.text: may extend the repayment period to no more than 18 months without Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The plan must state:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The amount of the partial reinstatement payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The total amount to be repaid
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Beginning and end dates of the repayment period
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The exact amount of the repayment plan payment that will be added to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s regular monthly payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That the Borrower must pay all scheduled monthly payments during the period of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That the Borrower must continue monthly payments according to the terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note at the end of the plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower agrees that the foreclosure proceedings are suspended during the
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment plan and that the Servicer is entitled to recommence foreclosure at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: point the foreclosure was suspended if the Borrower defaults on the repayment plan

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: must not include reimbursement for costs the Servicer incurred if State law prohibits

elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Mortgage, the Servicer must submit the plan on Form 105 to Freddie Mac (see

elements.text: Directory 5) for consideration.

elements.text: (f) Reporting and processing the reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Processing partial reinstatements and repayment plans
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Additional requirements for Mortgages with buydown funds

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When processing a reinstatement for a Mortgage subject to a temporary subsidy

elements.text: buydown plan in accordance with Section 4204.3, the Servicer must not apply funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: in a buydown account to bring a delinquent Mortgage current unless it is required per

elements.text: the terms of the buydown agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Upon reinstatement of a Mortgage that is subject to a temporary subsidy buydown
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan, the Borrower must resume their monthly payment amount as specified in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: terms of the buydown agreement, subject to applicable law and the terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: buydown agreement*
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the buydown term has expired or if, for any reason, the buydown funds are not
elements.label: text

elements.text: available or the buydown funds are not paid, the Borrower is responsible to continue
elements.label: text

elements.text: to make full monthly Mortgage payments as required by the terms of the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any remaining funds held in association with a buydown account, upon the end of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: buydown term or the liquidation of the Mortgage, whichever occurs first, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must distribute in accordance with applicable law and per the buydown agreement or
elements.label: text

elements.text: apply to the Mortgage consistent with Section 4204.3(e)
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: the terms of the buydown agreement, applicable law and the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Reimbursement of expenses related to reinstatements
elements.label: text

elements.text: If applicable federal, State or local law prohibits collection of incurred expenses (e.g., legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: fees and/or legal costs) from the Borrower upon reinstatement of the Mortgage, Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: will reimburse certain expenses the Servicer incurs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 9701.1(e)(iv) regarding unrecoverable expenses.

elements.text: 9203.2: Repayment plan requirements and reporting
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2026-2, which announced updates related to Resolve® reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for repayment plans and forbearance agreements. Servicers may implement
elements.label: text

elements.text: the new requirements prior to the mandatory October 1, 2026 version of this section if they
elements.label: text

elements.text: are operationally ready to do so.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ What is a repayment plan?
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Repayment plan requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Additional requirements for Mortgages with buydown funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer approval authority for repayment plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reporting repayment plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) What is a repayment plan?
elements.label: text

elements.text: A repayment plan is an agreement between the Servicer and a Borrower that gives the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower a defined period of time to reinstate the Mortgage by paying normal regular
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments plus an additional agreed upon amount in repayment of the Delinquency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Repayment plan requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Repayment plan requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Borrower to enter into a repayment plan, the Borrower must have the financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: capacity to bring the Mortgage current.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any repayment plan must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Lead to full reinstatement or payoff at the conclusion of the plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Be in writing and must specify:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The total amount to be repaid
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The beginning and end dates of the repayment period
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The exact amount of the repayment plan payment that will be added to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s regular monthly payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That the Borrower must pay all scheduled monthly payments during the period of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That the Borrower must continue monthly payments according to the terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note at the end of the plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That the Borrower agrees that the foreclosure proceedings are suspended during
elements.label: text

elements.text: the repayment plan and that the Servicer is entitled to recommence foreclosure at
elements.label: text

elements.text: the point the foreclosure was suspended, if applicable, if the Borrower defaults on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 93, Evaluation Notices, includes a model repayment plan that a Servicer may
elements.label: text

elements.text: use and amend as necessary to comply with the requirements of this section and
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Be sent to the Borrower. The document does not have to be signed by the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: or returned to the Servicer as a condition of the repayment plan. However, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower must make the first payment due if the Mortgage is in foreclosure at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: time the repayment plan offer is extended in order to accept the terms of the plan. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment agreement must then contain a stipulation that the Servicer is entitled to
elements.label: text

elements.text: recommence foreclosure at the point the foreclosure was suspended if the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: defaults on the repayment plan, unless otherwise prohibited by applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must keep as part of the Mortgage file:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The repayment plan (do not send Freddie Mac a copy)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The specifics of the repayment plan and the date the Servicer negotiated it with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When the Servicer sent it to the Borrower, if the plan exceeds three months
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The reason the Servicer agreed to the repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Have a term greater than one month and less than or equal to 12 months, unless a
elements.label: text

elements.text: greater term is approved by Freddie Mac in writing or otherwise permitted for
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment of delinquent taxes, as outlined in Section 9203.2(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The repayment plan may include any accrued late charges due from the Borrower at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: time the Servicer entered into the repayment plan with the Borrower. However, if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower is paying as stipulated in the agreement, the Servicer must not accrue or collect
elements.label: text

elements.text: late charges from the Borrower during the repayment plan. If the Borrower defaults on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the terms of the repayment plan, late charge accrual may recommence from the date the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower defaulted on the plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Failed repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Once a Borrower accepts a repayment plan, the Borrower must meet the terms specified
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the repayment plan agreement. The repayment plan has failed if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer determines that the Borrower has failed to meet the terms specified in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the repayment plan agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower no longer has the financial capacity to bring the Mortgage current
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower accepts a repayment plan and does not make the total monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment plan payment by the end of the month in which the repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment is due
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Failed repayment plan – solicitations
elements.label: text

elements.text: In order to promote a more seamless transition between loss mitigation options when a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower is unable to resolve their Delinquency with a repayment plan, the table below
elements.label: text

elements.text: provides requirements for Servicers to conduct reviews for proactive Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Freddie Mac Flex Modification® offers following an unsuccessful repayment plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Failed repayment plan – Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: If…. Then…
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower accepts an offer for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment plan but does not make the
elements.label: text

elements.text: total monthly repayment plan payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must evaluate the Borrower for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: proactive offer for a Payment Deferral in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the eligibility criteria
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the end of the month in which it is
elements.label: text

elements.text: due (“fails a repayment plan”), and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer is unable to establish quality
elements.label: text

elements.text: right party contact.
elements.label: text

elements.text: described below. If eligible, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: send the Borrower a proactive offer for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment Deferral by the 15th day of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following month (i.e., the 15th day of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: month following the month Borrower fails a
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment plan).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower must be eligible for a Payment Deferral in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of this chapter. However:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer is not required to have established quality right party contact, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Section 9203.4(c), and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer is not required to confirm that the Borrower meets the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility requirements described in Section 9203.4(c)(i)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Solicitation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must solicit the Borrower using Exhibit 1105, Payment Deferral Post￾Repayment Plan Solicitation Cover Letter, with Exhibit 1100, Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agreement, or its equivalent, making any appropriate changes to comply with
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law. While use of Exhibits 1105 and 1100 is optional, it reflects the
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum level of information that the Servicer must communicate and illustrates a
elements.label: text

elements.text: level of specificity that complies with the requirements of the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must include instruction on how to accept the offer in the Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Deferral agreement. The Servicer is authorized to consider the following as acceptance
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Borrower, subject to applicable law:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower contacting the Servicer directly in accordance with any acceptable
elements.label: text

elements.text: outreach and communication method
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower returning an executed Payment Deferral agreement, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any other method evidencing the Borrower’s acceptance as determined by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower must make their full monthly contractual payment during the month of
elements.label: text

elements.text: solicitation if, as of the date of evaluation:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is 6 months delinquent, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Payment Deferral would cause the Mortgage to exceed 12 months of
elements.label: text

elements.text: cumulative deferred past-due principal and interest payments resulting from non￾disaster Payment Deferrals
elements.label: text

elements.text: In these circumstances, the Servicer must complete the Payment Deferral within the
elements.label: text

elements.text: month of the solicitation after receipt of the Borrower’s full monthly contractual
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment due during that month.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the Servicer uses a processing month to complete the Payment Deferral, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower must also make his or her full monthly contractual payment during the
elements.label: text

elements.text: processing month. The Servicer must complete the Payment Deferral within the
elements.label: text

elements.text: processing month after receipt of the Borrower’s full monthly contractual payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: during that month.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Flex Modification in accordance with the requirements of Section
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Solicitation Cover Letter, and the Exhibit 93 template for the Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower Response Package evaluation notice, or its equivalent. Exhibits 1191 and 93
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be altered at the Servicer’s discretion as it deems necessary to meet the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of this section and Chapter 9206 and to comply with disclosure and other
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements under applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Additional requirements for Mortgages with buydown funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: The additional requirements for Mortgages with buydown funds are as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When processing repayment plans for Mortgages subject to a temporary subsidy
elements.label: text

elements.text: buydown plan in accordance with Section 4204.3, the Servicer must not apply funds in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: buydown account to reduce the total amount to be repaid in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of a repayment plan as specified in Section 9203.2(b)(i) unless it is required
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the terms of the applicable buydown agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Upon entering a repayment plan, the Borrower must resume their payment amount as
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified in the terms of the buydown agreement, subject to applicable law and the terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the buydown agreement. In addition, the Borrower must pay the amount required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: resolve the Delinquency within the agreed-upon repayment plan term.*
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the buydown term has expired or if, for any reason, the buydown funds are not
elements.label: text

elements.text: available or the buydown funds are not paid, the Borrower is responsible to continue to
elements.label: text

elements.text: make full monthly Mortgage payments as required by the terms of the Note (plus the
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount required to resolve the delinquency within the agreed-upon repayment plan term,
elements.label: text

elements.text: if any such amounts remain).
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In addition to the repayment plan requirements in Section 9203.2(b)(i), the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: make the appropriate changes to the evaluation notice as necessary to reflect any
elements.label: text

elements.text: buydown fund contributions and the Borrower's portion of the payment in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the buydown agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any remaining funds held in association with a buydown account, upon the end of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: buydown term or the liquidation of the Mortgage, whichever occurs first, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must distribute in accordance with applicable law and per the buydown agreement or
elements.label: text

elements.text: apply to the Mortgage consistent with Section 4204.3(e)
elements.label: text

elements.text: * Application of each monthly payment must continue to be applied in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: terms of the buydown agreement, applicable law and the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Servicer approval authority for repayment plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may use Resolve® to report repayment plans to Freddie Mac. Servicers should
elements.label: text

elements.text: refer to Resolve Online Help for details on the use of the Resolve to report repayment plans.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: When repayment plans are submitted via Resolve, the system will report the associated
elements.label: text

elements.text: EDR default action codes on behalf of the Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must obtain Freddie Mac’s approval for a repayment plan that exceeds 12
elements.label: text

elements.text: months in duration unless the repayment plan includes the repayment of delinquent taxes. To
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtain Freddie Mac’s approval, the Servicer must submit the following to Freddie Mac (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Directory 5):
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The complete Borrower Response Package. (See Section 9102.5 for a description of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower Response Package.)

elements.label: text

elements.text: than or equal to 12 months without obtaining Freddie Mac’s approval, as outlined in the table

elements.text: (e) Reporting repayment plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: In accordance with Section 9102.6, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notify Freddie Mac via an EDR transmission with specific repayment plan activity after
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer has entered into a repayment plan with a Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Receive a repayment plan payment to validate the repayment plan and report default
elements.label: text

elements.text: action code 12 (Repayment Plan) once the initial repayment plan payment is received
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Continue reporting this code for each month of the repayment period, except for the last
elements.label: text

elements.text: month of the repayment period in which the Mortgage will be fully reinstated
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Continue to report that the Borrower is in a repayment plan until the Borrower has fully
elements.label: text

elements.text: reinstated the Mortgage or the Borrower defaults on the repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower fails to make a payment due under the repayment plan, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: discontinue reporting default action code 12.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For additional information about EDR, refer to Section 9102.6.

elements.text: This section contains information related to:

elements.text: ■ Reporting repayment plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) What is a repayment plan?
elements.label: text

elements.text: A repayment plan is an agreement between the Servicer and a Borrower that gives the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower a defined period of time to reinstate the Mortgage by paying normal regular
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments plus an additional agreed upon amount in repayment of the Delinquency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Repayment plan requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Repayment plan requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Borrower to enter into a repayment plan, the Borrower must have the financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: capacity to bring the Mortgage current.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any repayment plan must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Lead to full reinstatement or payoff at the conclusion of the plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Be in writing and must specify:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The total amount to be repaid
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The beginning and end dates of the repayment period
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The exact amount of the repayment plan payment that will be added to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s regular monthly payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That the Borrower must pay all scheduled monthly payments during the period of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That the Borrower must continue monthly payments according to the terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note at the end of the plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That the Borrower agrees that the foreclosure proceedings are suspended during
elements.label: text

elements.text: the repayment plan and that the Servicer is entitled to recommence foreclosure at
elements.label: text

elements.text: the point the foreclosure was suspended, if applicable, if the Borrower defaults on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 93, Evaluation Notices, includes a model repayment plan that a Servicer may
elements.label: text

elements.text: use and amend as necessary to comply with the requirements of this section and
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Be sent to the Borrower. The document does not have to be signed by the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: or returned to the Servicer as a condition of the repayment plan. However, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower must make the first payment due if the Mortgage is in foreclosure at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: time the repayment plan offer is extended in order to accept the terms of the plan. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment agreement must then contain a stipulation that the Servicer is entitled to
elements.label: text

elements.text: recommence foreclosure at the point the foreclosure was suspended if the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: defaults on the repayment plan, unless otherwise prohibited by applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must keep as part of the Mortgage file:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The repayment plan (do not send Freddie Mac a copy)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The specifics of the repayment plan and the date the Servicer negotiated it with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When the Servicer sent it to the Borrower, if the plan exceeds three months
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The reason the Servicer agreed to the repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Have a term greater than one month and less than or equal to 12 months, unless a
elements.label: text

elements.text: greater term is approved by Freddie Mac in writing or otherwise permitted for
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment of delinquent taxes, as outlined in Section 9203.2(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The repayment plan may include any accrued late charges due from the Borrower at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: time the Servicer entered into the repayment plan with the Borrower. However, if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower is paying as stipulated in the agreement, the Servicer must not accrue or collect
elements.label: text

elements.text: late charges from the Borrower during the repayment plan. If the Borrower defaults on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the terms of the repayment plan, late charge accrual may recommence from the date the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower defaulted on the plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Failed repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Once a Borrower accepts a repayment plan, the Borrower must meet the terms specified
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the repayment plan agreement. The repayment plan has failed if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer determines that the Borrower has failed to meet the terms specified in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the repayment plan agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower no longer has the financial capacity to bring the Mortgage current
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower accepts a repayment plan and does not make the total monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment plan payment by the end of the month in which the repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment is due
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Failed repayment plan – solicitations
elements.label: text

elements.text: In order to promote a more seamless transition between loss mitigation options when a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower is unable to resolve their Delinquency with a repayment plan, the table below
elements.label: text

elements.text: provides requirements for Servicers to conduct reviews for proactive Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Freddie Mac Flex Modification® offers following an unsuccessful repayment plan.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer is unable to establish quality
elements.label: text

elements.text: right party contact.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: following month (i.e., the 15th day of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: month following the month Borrower fails a
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower must be eligible for a Payment Deferral in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of this chapter. However:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer is not required to have established quality right party contact, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Section 9203.4(c), and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer is not required to confirm that the Borrower meets the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility requirements described in Section 9203.4(c)(i)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Solicitation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must solicit the Borrower using Exhibit 1105, Payment Deferral Post￾Repayment Plan Solicitation Cover Letter, with Exhibit 1100, Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agreement, or its equivalent, making any appropriate changes to comply with
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law. While use of Exhibits 1105 and 1100 is optional, it reflects the
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum level of information that the Servicer must communicate and illustrates a
elements.label: text

elements.text: level of specificity that complies with the requirements of the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must include instruction on how to accept the offer in the Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Deferral agreement. The Servicer is authorized to consider the following as acceptance
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Borrower, subject to applicable law:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower contacting the Servicer directly in accordance with any acceptable
elements.label: text

elements.text: outreach and communication method
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower returning an executed Payment Deferral agreement, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any other method evidencing the Borrower’s acceptance as determined by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower must make their full monthly contractual payment during the month of
elements.label: text

elements.text: solicitation if, as of the date of evaluation:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is 6 months delinquent, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Payment Deferral would cause the Mortgage to exceed 12 months of
elements.label: text

elements.text: cumulative deferred past-due principal and interest payments resulting from non￾disaster Payment Deferrals
elements.label: text

elements.text: In these circumstances, the Servicer must complete the Payment Deferral within the
elements.label: text

elements.text: month of the solicitation after receipt of the Borrower’s full monthly contractual
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment due during that month.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the Servicer uses a processing month to complete the Payment Deferral, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower must also make his or her full monthly contractual payment during the
elements.label: text

elements.text: processing month. The Servicer must complete the Payment Deferral within the
elements.label: text

elements.text: processing month after receipt of the Borrower’s full monthly contractual payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: during that month.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Additional requirements for Mortgages with buydown funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: The additional requirements for Mortgages with buydown funds are as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When processing repayment plans for Mortgages subject to a temporary subsidy
elements.label: text

elements.text: buydown plan in accordance with Section 4204.3, the Servicer must not apply funds in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: buydown account to reduce the total amount to be repaid in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of a repayment plan as specified in Section 9203.2(b)(i) unless it is required
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the terms of the applicable buydown agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Upon entering a repayment plan, the Borrower must resume their payment amount as
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified in the terms of the buydown agreement, subject to applicable law and the terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the buydown agreement. In addition, the Borrower must pay the amount required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: resolve the Delinquency within the agreed-upon repayment plan term.*
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the buydown term has expired or if, for any reason, the buydown funds are not
elements.label: text

elements.text: available or the buydown funds are not paid, the Borrower is responsible to continue to
elements.label: text

elements.text: make full monthly Mortgage payments as required by the terms of the Note (plus the
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount required to resolve the delinquency within the agreed-upon repayment plan term,
elements.label: text

elements.text: if any such amounts remain).
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In addition to the repayment plan requirements in Section 9203.2(b)(i), the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: make the appropriate changes to the evaluation notice as necessary to reflect any
elements.label: text

elements.text: buydown fund contributions and the Borrower's portion of the payment in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the buydown agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any remaining funds held in association with a buydown account, upon the end of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: buydown term or the liquidation of the Mortgage, whichever occurs first, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must distribute in accordance with applicable law and per the buydown agreement or
elements.label: text

elements.text: apply to the Mortgage consistent with Section 4204.3(e)
elements.label: text

elements.text: * Application of each monthly payment must continue to be applied in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: terms of the buydown agreement, applicable law and the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Servicer approval authority for repayment plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must use Resolve® to report repayment plans to Freddie Mac. Servicers should
elements.label: text

elements.text: refer to Resolve Online Help for details on the use of the Resolve to report repayment plans.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: EDR default action codes on behalf of the Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: months in duration unless the repayment plan includes the repayment of delinquent taxes. To
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtain Freddie Mac’s approval, the Servicer must submit the following to Freddie Mac (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Directory 5):

elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The complete Borrower Response Package. (See Section 9102.5 for a description of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower Response Package.)

elements.label: text

elements.text: than or equal to 12 months without obtaining Freddie Mac’s approval, as outlined in the table

elements.text: 9203.3: Forbearance plans and requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2026-2, which announced updates related to Resolve® reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for repayment plans and forbearance agreements. Servicers may implement
elements.label: text

elements.text: the new requirements prior to the mandatory October 1, 2026 version of this section if they
elements.label: text

elements.text: are operationally ready to do so.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ What is a forbearance?
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requirements for a forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower contact requirements when transitioning from a forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrowers in a Trial Period Plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Forbearance plan termination
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other forbearance plan conditions and requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) What is a forbearance?
elements.label: text

elements.text: A forbearance plan is a written agreement between the Servicer and the Borrower (or
elements.label: text

elements.text: deceased Borrower’s estate) that reflects the terms of the forbearance, including whether the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower may make either reduced or no monthly payments for a specific period of time. If
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer achieves quality right party contact and the Borrower meets the eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: criteria for a forbearance plan in accordance with Section 9203.3(b), the Servicer must offer:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An initial forbearance term for a period of one to three months, and, if necessary
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ One or more forbearance term extensions of one to three months
elements.label: text

elements.text: The forbearance plan may not be extended beyond a date that would cause the Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: to exceed a cumulative total of 12 months of the Borrower’s contractual monthly Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment, including taxes and insurance, if the Servicer is collecting Escrow for those
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses, without prior approval from Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Prior to the expiration of the initial and any extension to the forbearance plan, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must evaluate the Borrower’s eligibility for an extension of the initial forbearance plan based
elements.label: text

elements.text: on quality right party contact. Each forbearance plan evaluation, including an evaluation for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan extension, must be completed in accordance with all eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in this section and Sections 9203.3(b) through 9203.3(f). Additionally, there is no
elements.label: text

elements.text: limit on the number of forbearance plans that an eligible Borrower can receive over the life
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 93, Evaluation Notices, includes forbearance agreements that a Servicer may use but
elements.label: text

elements.text: must amend as necessary to comply with the requirements of this section, Sections 9203.3(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: through 9203.3(f) and applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: At the end of the forbearance period, the Borrower must cure the Delinquency through one of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following options:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Full reinstatement of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Partial reinstatement followed by a repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Payment of the Mortgage in full
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A loan modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pay off the Mortgage through a short sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: A loan modification is a type of workout option that enables the Borrower to retain
elements.label: text

elements.text: homeownership (see Section 9201.6). If the Borrower can no longer afford to retain the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property, the Servicer must pursue a workout option to liquidate the Borrower’s interest in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the property (see Section 9201.6), such as a short sale, deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, a workout
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage assumption, or a simultaneous modification and assumption.

elements.text: (b) Requirements for a forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may use Resolve® to report forbearance plans to Freddie Mac. Servicers should
elements.label: text

elements.text: refer to Resolve Online Help for details on the use of the Resolve to report forbearance
elements.label: text

elements.text: plans.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: When forbearance plans are submitted via Resolve, the system will report the
elements.label: text

elements.text: associated EDR default action codes on behalf of the Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Borrower eligibility requirements for a forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be eligible for a forbearance plan, the Borrower:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must have an eligible hardship as described in Section 9202.1(b) and Form 710,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage Assistance Application. The Borrower’s hardship may be verbally stated.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must report the hardship reason via EDR in accordance with Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ May be either current or delinquent, but the forbearance plan must not result in an
elements.label: text

elements.text: overall Mortgage Delinquency that exceeds 360 days (i.e., to be in an active
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan, the Borrower must not have missed more than 12 contractual
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly Mortgage payments)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: No Borrower documentation or written application (e.g., Form 710 or a Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: customized equivalent of Form 710) is required from the Borrower; however, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must achieve quality right party contact in accordance with the requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9102.3(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Mortgage eligibility and exclusions for a forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by the Borrower’s Primary Residence, even if vacant, are eligible for
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following Mortgages are ineligible for forbearance:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages secured by abandoned or condemned properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages secured by second homes or Investment Properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Forbearance plan agreement with the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must determine the terms of the forbearance agreement based on
elements.label: text

elements.text: discussions with the Borrower. The forbearance agreement:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must be provided to the Borrower in writing and signed by the Servicer. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: document does not have to be signed by the Borrower or returned to the Servicer as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: condition of the forbearance plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must indicate the duration of the forbearance plan, including the effective date, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: expiration date of the forbearance plan and, if applicable, the due date of the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment and the amount of such payment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 9203.3(b)(iv) for additional information about reduced payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: (if applicable) under a forbearance plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must state that, at the end of the forbearance period, the Borrower must either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Cure the Delinquency through a full reinstatement, partial reinstatement plus
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment plan or a repayment plan, or payoff of the Mortgage, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Submit a complete Borrower Response package to be evaluated for a workout
elements.label: text

elements.text: option prior to the conclusion of the plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Sections 9203.1(a) through 9203.2(d) for requirements on
elements.label: text

elements.text: reinstatements and repayment plans and to Chapters 9206 through 9210 for
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements on workout options.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must state that foreclosure proceedings are suspended during the forbearance period
elements.label: text

elements.text: so long as the Borrower complies with the forbearance agreement and that, unless
elements.label: text

elements.text: prohibited by applicable law, the Servicer may recommence foreclosure at the point it
elements.label: text

elements.text: was suspended if the Borrower defaults on the forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ May include a requirement to pay any accrued late charges due from the Borrower at
elements.label: text

elements.text: the time the Servicer entered into the forbearance agreement with the Borrower. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must not accrue or collect late charges from the Borrower during the
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance period, or any subsequent repayment plan period, if the Borrower is
elements.label: text

elements.text: complying with the terms of such agreements. If the Borrower defaults on the terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the forbearance agreement, late charge accrual may recommence from the date the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower defaulted on the agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Reduced payments under a forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a monthly payment is required under the terms of a forbearance plan:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Such payment must be less than the Borrower’s monthly contractual Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment, including, as applicable, principal, interest and Escrow payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must receive such payment from the Borrower on or before the last day
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the month in which it is due. If the Borrower fails to make timely forbearance
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments, the forbearance plan must be canceled unless the Servicer determines that
elements.label: text

elements.text: there are mitigating circumstances that caused the payment to be late.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If there are mitigating circumstances that prevents the Borrower from making the
elements.label: text

elements.text: required reduced payment, the Servicer may determine whether to amend the
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan to further reduce the required payment or allow the Borrower to make
elements.label: text

elements.text: no payments under a new forbearance plan agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower fails to make timely payments and there are no mitigating
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evaluate the Borrower for other alternatives to foreclosure (e.g., solicit the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: for a complete Borrower Response Package
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Conduct an evaluation for a streamlined offering of the Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification) and otherwise, pursue foreclosure in accordance with Chapter 9301.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Use good business judgment to determine whether forbearance payments were
elements.label: text

elements.text: received timely or if mitigating circumstances (such as a further reduction in income)
elements.label: text

elements.text: caused the payment to be late. Exceptions to the timely payment requirement must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: documented and retained in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Borrower contact requirements when transitioning from a forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Contact requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer has discretion to determine the appropriate frequency to contact the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower. However, the Servicer must initiate contact no later than 30 days prior to any
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan end date. Borrower contact must continue until quality right party
elements.label: text

elements.text: contact is achieved or until the forbearance plan has expired.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If quality right party contact is achieved, the Servicer must determine whether:
